tolerance: <int-or-string> # intOrString","description":"tolerance is the tolerance on the ratio between the current and desired\nmetric value under which no updates are made to the desired number of\nreplicas (e.g. 0.01 for 1%). Must be greater than or equal to zero. If not\nset, the default cluster-wide tolerance is applied (by default 10%).\n\nFor example, if autoscaling is configured with a memory consumption target of 100Mi,\nand scale-down and scale-up tolerances of 5% and 1% respectively, scaling will be\ntriggered when the actual consumption falls below 95Mi or exceeds 101Mi.\n\nThis is an alpha field and requires enabling the HPAConfigurableTolerance\nfeature gate.","depth":4,"field":"tolerance","path":"spec.autoscaling.behavior.scaleDown.tolerance","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-autoscaling-behavior-scaledown-tolerance"},{"index":152,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-152"},{"index":153,"text":"      # scaleUp is scaling policy for scaling Up. If not set, the default value","description":"scaleUp is scaling policy for scaling Up. optional, listType:","description":"The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. The node that is\nmost preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.\nfor each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource\nrequest, requiredDuringScheduling affinity expressions, etc.),\ncompute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and adding\n\"weight\" to the sum if the node matches the corresponding matchExpressions; the\nnode(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.","depth":4,"field":"preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-nodeaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution"},{"index":776,"text":"                                                         # atomic","description":"The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. The node that is\nmost preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.\nfor each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource\nrequest, requiredDuringScheduling affinity expressions, etc.),\ncompute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and adding\n\"weight\" to the sum if the node matches the corresponding matchExpressions; the\nnode(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","metadata":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-nodeaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution"},{"index":777,"text":"          - optional, listType:","description":"The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. The node that is\nmost preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.\nfor each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource\nrequest, requiredDuringScheduling affinity expressions, etc.),\ncompute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and adding\n\"weight\" to the sum if the node has pods which matches the corresponding podAffinityTerm; the\nnode(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.","depth":4,"field":"preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.podAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-podaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution"},{"index":878,"text":"                                                         # atomic","description":"The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. optional, listType:","description":"The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe anti-affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. The node that is\nmost preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.\nfor each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource\nrequest, requiredDuringScheduling anti-affinity expressions, etc.),\ncompute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and subtracting\n\"weight\" from the sum if the node has pods which matches the corresponding podAffinityTerm; the\nnode(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.","depth":4,"field":"preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-podantiaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution"},{"index":1118,"text":"                                                         # atomic","description":"The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe anti-affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. The node that is\nmost preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.\nfor each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource\nrequest, requiredDuringScheduling anti-affinity expressions, etc.),\ncompute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and subtracting\n\"weight\" from the sum if the node has pods which matches the corresponding podAffinityTerm; the\nnode(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","metadata":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-podantiaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution"},{"index":1119,"text":"          - # Required. The keys are used to lookup values from the\nincoming pod labels, those key-value labels are merged with `labelSelector` as `key in (value)`\nto select the group of existing pods which pods will be taken into consideration\nfor the incoming pod's pod (anti) affinity. Keys that don't exist in the incoming\npod labels will be ignored. optional, listType:","description":"If the anti-affinity requirements specified by this field are not met at\nscheduling time, the pod will not be scheduled onto the node.\nIf the anti-affinity requirements specified by this field cease to be met\nat some point during pod execution (e.g. due to a pod label update), the\nsystem may or may not try to eventually evict the pod from its node.\nWhen there are multiple elements, the lists of nodes corresponding to each\npodAffinityTerm are intersected, i.e. all terms must be satisfied.","depth":4,"field":"requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-podantiaffinity-requiredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution"},{"index":1240,"text":"                                                        # The keys are used to lookup values from the\nincoming pod labels, those key-value labels are merged with `labelSelector` as `key in (value)`\nto select the group of existing pods which pods will be taken into consideration\nfor the incoming pod's pod (anti) affinity. Keys that don't exist in the incoming\npod labels will be ignored. The keys are used to lookup values from the\nincoming pod labels, those key-value labels are merged with `labelSelector` as `key notin (value)`\nto select the group of existing pods which pods will be taken into consideration\nfor the incoming pod's pod (anti) affinity. Keys that don't exist in the incoming\npod labels will be ignored. The default value is empty.\nThe same key is forbidden to exist in both mismatchLabelKeys and labelSelector.\nAlso, mismatchLabelKeys cannot be set when labelSelector isn't set.","depth":6,"field":"mismatchLabelKeys","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].mismatchLabelKeys","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-podantiaffinity-requiredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-mismatchlabelkeys"},{"index":1302,"text":"              # The Pod's termination grace period countdown begins before the\nPreStop hook is executed. Regardless of the outcome of the handler, the\ncontainer will eventually terminate within the Pod's termination grace\nperiod (unless delayed by finalizers). Other management of the container blocks until the hook completes\nor until the termination grace period is reached.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks","depth":5,"field":"preStop","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].lifecycle.preStop","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-lifecycle-prestop"},{"index":1613,"text":"            # Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.","description":"Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.","depth":6,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].lifecycle.preStop.exec","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-lifecycle-prestop-exec"},{"index":1614,"text":"            # exec:","description":"Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.","depth":6,"field":"exec","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].lifecycle.preStop.exec","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-lifecycle-prestop-exec"},{"index":1615,"text":"              # Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the","description":"Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the","depth":7,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].lifecycle.preStop.exec.command","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-lifecycle-prestop-exec-command"},{"index":1616,"text":"              # working directory for the command is root ('/') in the","description":"working directory for the command is root ('/') in the","depth":7,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].lifecycle.preStop.exec.command","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-lifecycle-prestop-exec-command"},{"index":1617,"text":"              # container's filesystem. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":5,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].livenessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-livenessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":1770,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-1770"},{"index":1771,"text":"          # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":5,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":1900,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-1900"},{"index":1901,"text":"          # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. When this field is not specified,\nthe restart behavior is defined by the Pod's restart policy and the container type.\nAdditionally, setting the RestartPolicy as \"Always\" for the init container will\nhave the following effect:\nthis init container will be continually restarted on\nexit until all regular containers have terminated. Once all regular\ncontainers have completed, all init containers with restartPolicy \"Always\"\nwill be shut down. This lifecycle differs from normal init containers and\nis often referred to as a \"sidecar\" container. Although this init\ncontainer still starts in the init container sequence, it does not wait\nfor the container to complete before proceeding to the next init\ncontainer. Instead, the next init container starts immediately after this\ninit container is started, or after any startupProbe has successfully\ncompleted.","depth":4,"field":"restartPolicy","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].restartPolicy","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-restartpolicy"},{"index":1967,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-1967"},{"index":1968,"text":"        # Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the container","description":"Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the container","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].restartPolicyRules","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-restartpolicyrules"},{"index":1969,"text":"        # should be restarted on exit. The rules are evaluated in\norder. Once a rule matches a container exit condition, the remaining\nrules are ignored. If no rule matches the container exit condition,\nthe Container-level restart policy determines the whether the container\nis restarted or not. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":5,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":2250,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-2250"},{"index":2251,"text":"          # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. recursiveReadOnly: \"<string>\"","description":"RecursiveReadOnly specifies whether read-only mounts should be handled\nrecursively.\n\nIf ReadOnly is false, this field has no meaning and must be unspecified.\n\nIf ReadOnly is true, and this field is set to Disabled, the mount is not made\nrecursively read-only.  If this field is set to IfPossible, the mount is made\nrecursively read-only, if it is supported by the container runtime.  If this\nfield is set to Enabled, the mount is made recursively read-only if it is\nsupported by the container runtime, otherwise the pod will not be started and\nan error will be generated to indicate the reason.\n\nIf this field is set to IfPossible or Enabled, MountPropagation must be set to\nNone (or be unspecified, which defaults to None).\n\nIf this field is not specified, it is treated as an equivalent of Disabled.","depth":6,"field":"recursiveReadOnly","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].volumeMounts[].recursiveReadOnly","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-volumemounts-recursivereadonly"},{"index":2346,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-2346"},{"index":2347,"text":"            # Path within the volume from which the container's volume should be","description":"Path within the volume from which the container's volume should be","depth":6,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].volumeMounts[].subPath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-volumemounts-subpath"},{"index":2348,"text":"            # mounted. The Pod's termination grace period countdown begins before the\nPreStop hook is executed. Regardless of the outcome of the handler, the\ncontainer will eventually terminate within the Pod's termination grace\nperiod (unless delayed by finalizers). Other management of the container blocks until the hook completes\nor until the termination grace period is reached.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks","depth":5,"field":"preStop","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].lifecycle.preStop","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-lifecycle-prestop"},{"index":2666,"text":"            # Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.","description":"Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.","depth":6,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].lifecycle.preStop.exec","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-lifecycle-prestop-exec"},{"index":2667,"text":"            # exec:","description":"Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.","depth":6,"field":"exec","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].lifecycle.preStop.exec","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-lifecycle-prestop-exec"},{"index":2668,"text":"              # Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the","description":"Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the","depth":7,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].lifecycle.preStop.exec.command","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-lifecycle-prestop-exec-command"},{"index":2669,"text":"              # working directory for the command is root ('/') in the","description":"working directory for the command is root ('/') in the","depth":7,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].lifecycle.preStop.exec.command","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-lifecycle-prestop-exec-command"},{"index":2670,"text":"              # container's filesystem. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":5,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].livenessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-livenessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":2821,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-2821"},{"index":2822,"text":"          # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":5,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].readinessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-readinessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":2942,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-2942"},{"index":2943,"text":"          # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. recursiveReadOnly: \"<string>\"","description":"RecursiveReadOnly specifies whether read-only mounts should be handled\nrecursively.\n\nIf ReadOnly is false, this field has no meaning and must be unspecified.\n\nIf ReadOnly is true, and this field is set to Disabled, the mount is not made\nrecursively read-only.  If this field is set to IfPossible, the mount is made\nrecursively read-only, if it is supported by the container runtime.  If this\nfield is set to Enabled, the mount is made recursively read-only if it is\nsupported by the container runtime, otherwise the pod will not be started and\nan error will be generated to indicate the reason.\n\nIf this field is set to IfPossible or Enabled, MountPropagation must be set to\nNone (or be unspecified, which defaults to None).\n\nIf this field is not specified, it is treated as an equivalent of Disabled.","depth":6,"field":"recursiveReadOnly","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].volumeMounts[].recursiveReadOnly","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-volumemounts-recursivereadonly"},{"index":3371,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-3371"},{"index":3372,"text":"            # Path within the volume from which the container's volume should be","description":"Path within the volume from which the container's volume should be","depth":6,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].volumeMounts[].subPath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-volumemounts-subpath"},{"index":3373,"text":"            # mounted. Setting false is useful for\nmitigating container breakout vulnerabilities even allowing users to run their\ncontainers as root without actually having root privileges on the host.\nThis field is alpha-level and is only honored by servers that enable the UserNamespacesSupport feature.","depth":2,"field":"hostUsers","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.hostUsers","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-hostusers"},{"index":3422,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-3422"},{"index":3423,"text":"    # Specifies the hostname of the Pod If not specified, the pod's hostname","description":"Specifies the hostname of the Pod If not specified, the pod's hostname","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.hostname","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-hostname"},{"index":3424,"text":"    # will be set to a system-defined value.","description":"will be set to a system-defined value.","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.hostname","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-hostname"},{"index":3425,"text":"    # hostname: \"<string>\"","description":"Specifies the hostname of the Pod\nIf not specified, the pod's hostname will be set to a system-defined value.","depth":2,"field":"hostname","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.hostname","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-hostname"},{"index":3426,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-3426"},{"index":3427,"text":"    # HostnameOverride specifies an explicit override for the pod's hostname as","description":"HostnameOverride specifies an explicit override for the pod's hostname as","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.hostnameOverride","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-hostnameoverride"},{"index":3428,"text":"    # perceived by the pod. Requires the HostnameOverride feature gate","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.hostnameOverride","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-hostnameoverride"},{"index":3437,"text":"    # to be enabled.","description":"to be enabled.","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.hostnameOverride","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-hostnameoverride"},{"index":3438,"text":"    # hostnameOverride: \"<string>\"","description":"HostnameOverride specifies an explicit override for the pod's hostname as perceived by the pod.\nThis field only specifies the pod's hostname and does not affect its DNS records.\nWhen this field is set to a non-empty string:\n- It takes precedence over the values set in `hostname` and `subdomain`.\n- The Pod's hostname will be set to this value.\n- `setHostnameAsFQDN` must be nil or set to false.\n- `hostNetwork` must be set to false.\n\nThis field must be a valid DNS subdomain as defined in RFC 1123 and contain at most 64 characters.\nRequires the HostnameOverride feature gate to be enabled.","depth":2,"field":"hostnameOverride","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.hostnameOverride","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-hostnameoverride"},{"index":3439,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-3439"},{"index":3440,"text":"    # ImagePullSecrets is an optional list of references to secrets in the same","description":"ImagePullSecrets is an optional list of references to secrets in the same","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.imagePullSecrets","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-imagepullsecrets"},{"index":3441,"text":"    # namespace to use for pulling any of the images used by this PodSpec. If any\ninit container fails, the pod is considered to have failed and is handled according\nto its restartPolicy. The name for an init container or normal container must be\nunique among all containers.\nInit containers may not have Lifecycle actions, Readiness probes, Liveness probes, or Startup probes.\nThe resourceRequirements of an init container are taken into account during scheduling\nby finding the highest request/limit for each resource type, and then using the max of\nthat value or the sum of the normal containers. Limits are applied to init containers\nin a similar fashion.\nInit containers cannot currently be added or removed.\nCannot be updated.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/init-containers/","depth":2,"field":"initContainers","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.initContainers","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-initcontainers"},{"index":3466,"text":"      - # Name of the container specified as a DNS_LABEL. The Pod's termination grace period countdown begins before the\nPreStop hook is executed. Regardless of the outcome of the handler, the\ncontainer will eventually terminate within the Pod's termination grace\nperiod (unless delayed by finalizers). Other management of the container blocks until the hook completes\nor until the termination grace period is reached.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks","depth":5,"field":"preStop","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.initContainers[].lifecycle.preStop","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-initcontainers-lifecycle-prestop"},{"index":3727,"text":"            # Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.","description":"Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.","depth":6,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.initContainers[].lifecycle.preStop.exec","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-initcontainers-lifecycle-prestop-exec"},{"index":3728,"text":"            # exec:","description":"Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.","depth":6,"field":"exec","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.initContainers[].lifecycle.preStop.exec","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-initcontainers-lifecycle-prestop-exec"},{"index":3729,"text":"              # Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the","description":"Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the","depth":7,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.initContainers[].lifecycle.preStop.exec.command","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-initcontainers-lifecycle-prestop-exec-command"},{"index":3730,"text":"              # working directory for the command is root ('/') in the","description":"working directory for the command is root ('/') in the","depth":7,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.initContainers[].lifecycle.preStop.exec.command","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-initcontainers-lifecycle-prestop-exec-command"},{"index":3731,"text":"              # container's filesystem. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":5,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.initContainers[].readinessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-initcontainers-readinessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":4014,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4014"},{"index":4015,"text":"          # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. When this field is not specified,\nthe restart behavior is defined by the Pod's restart policy and the container type.\nAdditionally, setting the RestartPolicy as \"Always\" for the init container will\nhave the following effect:\nthis init container will be continually restarted on\nexit until all regular containers have terminated. Once all regular\ncontainers have completed, all init containers with restartPolicy \"Always\"\nwill be shut down. This lifecycle differs from normal init containers and\nis often referred to as a \"sidecar\" container. Although this init\ncontainer still starts in the init container sequence, it does not wait\nfor the container to complete before proceeding to the next init\ncontainer. Instead, the next init container starts immediately after this\ninit container is started, or after any startupProbe has successfully\ncompleted.","depth":4,"field":"restartPolicy","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.initContainers[].restartPolicy","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-initcontainers-restartpolicy"},{"index":4081,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4081"},{"index":4082,"text":"        # Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the container","description":"Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the container","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.initContainers[].restartPolicyRules","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-initcontainers-restartpolicyrules"},{"index":4083,"text":"        # should be restarted on exit. The rules are evaluated in\norder. Once a rule matches a container exit condition, the remaining\nrules are ignored. If no rule matches the container exit condition,\nthe Container-level restart policy determines the whether the container\nis restarted or not. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. recursiveReadOnly: \"<string>\"","description":"RecursiveReadOnly specifies whether read-only mounts should be handled\nrecursively.\n\nIf ReadOnly is false, this field has no meaning and must be unspecified.\n\nIf ReadOnly is true, and this field is set to Disabled, the mount is not made\nrecursively read-only.  If this field is set to IfPossible, the mount is made\nrecursively read-only, if it is supported by the container runtime.  If this\nfield is set to Enabled, the mount is made recursively read-only if it is\nsupported by the container runtime, otherwise the pod will not be started and\nan error will be generated to indicate the reason.\n\nIf this field is set to IfPossible or Enabled, MountPropagation must be set to\nNone (or be unspecified, which defaults to None).\n\nIf this field is not specified, it is treated as an equivalent of Disabled.","depth":6,"field":"recursiveReadOnly","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.initContainers[].volumeMounts[].recursiveReadOnly","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-initcontainers-volumemounts-recursivereadonly"},{"index":4460,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4460"},{"index":4461,"text":"            # Path within the volume from which the container's volume should be","description":"Path within the volume from which the container's volume should be","depth":6,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.initContainers[].volumeMounts[].subPath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-initcontainers-volumemounts-subpath"},{"index":4462,"text":"            # mounted. Escaped references will never be expanded, regardless\nof whether the variable exists or not. Not executed within a shell.\nThe container image's ENTRYPOINT is used if this is not provided.\nVariable references $(VAR_NAME) are expanded using the container's environment. If a variable\ncannot be resolved, the reference in the input string will be unchanged. Double $$ are reduced\nto a single $, which allows for escaping the $(VAR_NAME) syntax: i.e. \"$$(VAR_NAME)\" will\nproduce the string literal \"$(VAR_NAME)\". Escaped references will never be expanded, regardless\nof whether the variable exists or not. The Pod's termination grace period countdown begins before the\nPreStop hook is executed. Regardless of the outcome of the handler, the\ncontainer will eventually terminate within the Pod's termination grace\nperiod (unless delayed by finalizers). Other management of the container blocks until the hook completes\nor until the termination grace period is reached.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks","depth":4,"field":"preStop","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.lifecycle.preStop","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-lifecycle-prestop"},{"index":4732,"text":"          # Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.","description":"Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.","depth":5,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.lifecycle.preStop.exec","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-lifecycle-prestop-exec"},{"index":4733,"text":"          # exec:","description":"Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.","depth":5,"field":"exec","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.lifecycle.preStop.exec","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-lifecycle-prestop-exec"},{"index":4734,"text":"            # Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the","description":"Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the","depth":6,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.lifecycle.preStop.exec.command","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-lifecycle-prestop-exec-command"},{"index":4735,"text":"            # working directory for the command is root ('/') in the container's","description":"working directory for the command is root ('/') in the container's","depth":6,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.lifecycle.preStop.exec.command","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-lifecycle-prestop-exec-command"},{"index":4736,"text":"            # filesystem. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. Minimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.readinessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-readinessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":5013,"text":"        # is used if unset.","description":"is used if unset.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.readinessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-readinessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":5014,"text":"        # terminationGracePeriodSeconds: <int64>","description":"Optional duration in seconds the pod needs to terminate gracefully upon probe failure.\nThe grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes running in the pod are sent\na termination signal and the time when the processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal.\nSet this value longer than the expected cleanup time for your process.\nIf this value is nil, the pod's terminationGracePeriodSeconds will be used. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":4,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.readinessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-readinessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":5015,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5015"},{"index":5016,"text":"        # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. When this field is not specified,\nthe restart behavior is defined by the Pod's restart policy and the container type.\nAdditionally, setting the RestartPolicy as \"Always\" for the init container will\nhave the following effect:\nthis init container will be continually restarted on\nexit until all regular containers have terminated. Once all regular\ncontainers have completed, all init containers with restartPolicy \"Always\"\nwill be shut down. This lifecycle differs from normal init containers and\nis often referred to as a \"sidecar\" container. Although this init\ncontainer still starts in the init container sequence, it does not wait\nfor the container to complete before proceeding to the next init\ncontainer. Instead, the next init container starts immediately after this\ninit container is started, or after any startupProbe has successfully\ncompleted.","depth":3,"field":"restartPolicy","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.restartPolicy","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-restartpolicy"},{"index":5081,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5081"},{"index":5082,"text":"      # Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the container","description":"Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the container","depth":3,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.restartPolicyRules","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-restartpolicyrules"},{"index":5083,"text":"      # should be restarted on exit. The rules are evaluated in\norder. Once a rule matches a container exit condition, the remaining\nrules are ignored. If no rule matches the container exit condition,\nthe Container-level restart policy determines the whether the container\nis restarted or not. Constraints on the rules:\n- At most 20 rules are allowed.\n- Rules can have the same action.\n- Identical rules are not forbidden in validations.\nWhen rules are specified, container MUST set RestartPolicy explicitly\neven it if matches the Pod's RestartPolicy.","depth":3,"field":"restartPolicyRules","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.restartPolicyRules","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-restartpolicyrules"},{"index":5092,"text":"        - # Specifies the action taken on a container exit if the requirements","description":"Specifies the action taken on a container exit if the requirements","depth":5,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.restartPolicyRules[].action","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-restartpolicyrules-action"},{"index":5093,"text":"          # are satisfied. optional","description":"StartupProbe indicates that the Pod has successfully initialized.\nIf specified, no other probes are executed until this completes successfully.\nIf this probe fails, the Pod will be restarted, just as if the livenessProbe failed.\nThis can be used to provide different probe parameters at the beginning of a Pod's lifecycle,\nwhen it might take a long time to load data or warm a cache, than during steady-state operation.\nThis cannot be updated.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes","depth":3,"field":"startupProbe","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.startupProbe","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-startupprobe"},{"index":5271,"text":"        # Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":4,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.startupProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-startupprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":5360,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5360"},{"index":5361,"text":"        # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. If stdinOnce is set to true, stdin is opened on container start, is empty until the\nfirst client attaches to stdin, and then remains open and accepts data until the client disconnects,\nat which time stdin is closed and remains closed until the container is restarted. If this\nflag is false, a container processes that reads from stdin will never receive an EOF.\nDefault is false","depth":3,"field":"stdinOnce","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.stdinOnce","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-stdinonce"},{"index":5381,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5381"},{"index":5382,"text":"      # Optional: Path at which the file to which the container's termination","description":"Optional: Path at which the file to which the container's termination","depth":3,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.terminationMessagePath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-terminationmessagepath"},{"index":5383,"text":"      # message will be written is mounted into the container's filesystem.","description":"message will be written is mounted into the container's filesystem.","depth":3,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.terminationMessagePath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-terminationmessagepath"},{"index":5384,"text":"      # Message written is intended to be brief final status, such as an","description":"Message written is intended to be brief final status, such as an","depth":3,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.terminationMessagePath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-terminationmessagepath"},{"index":5385,"text":"      # assertion failure message. recursiveReadOnly: \"<string>\"","description":"RecursiveReadOnly specifies whether read-only mounts should be handled\nrecursively.\n\nIf ReadOnly is false, this field has no meaning and must be unspecified.\n\nIf ReadOnly is true, and this field is set to Disabled, the mount is not made\nrecursively read-only.  If this field is set to IfPossible, the mount is made\nrecursively read-only, if it is supported by the container runtime.  If this\nfield is set to Enabled, the mount is made recursively read-only if it is\nsupported by the container runtime, otherwise the pod will not be started and\nan error will be generated to indicate the reason.\n\nIf this field is set to IfPossible or Enabled, MountPropagation must be set to\nNone (or be unspecified, which defaults to None).\n\nIf this field is not specified, it is treated as an equivalent of Disabled.","depth":5,"field":"recursiveReadOnly","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.volumeMounts[].recursiveReadOnly","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-volumemounts-recursivereadonly"},{"index":5453,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5453"},{"index":5454,"text":"          # Path within the volume from which the container's volume should be","description":"Path within the volume from which the container's volume should be","depth":5,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.mainContainer.volumeMounts[].subPath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-maincontainer-volumemounts-subpath"},{"index":5455,"text":"          # mounted. optional","description":"Specifies the OS of the containers in the pod.\nSome pod and container fields are restricted if this is set.\n\nIf the OS field is set to linux, the following fields must be unset:\n-securityContext.windowsOptions\n\nIf the OS field is set to windows, following fields must be unset:\n- spec.hostPID\n- spec.hostIPC\n- spec.hostUsers\n- spec.resources\n- spec.securityContext.appArmorProfile\n- spec.securityContext.seLinuxOptions\n- spec.securityContext.seccompProfile\n- spec.securityContext.fsGroup\n- spec.securityContext.fsGroupChangePolicy\n- spec.securityContext.sysctls\n- spec.shareProcessNamespace\n- spec.securityContext.runAsUser\n- spec.securityContext.runAsGroup\n- spec.securityContext.supplementalGroups\n- spec.securityContext.supplementalGroupsPolicy\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.appArmorProfile\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.seLinuxOptions\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.seccompProfile\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.capabilities\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.privileged\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.procMount\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.runAsUser\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.runAsGroup","depth":2,"field":"os","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.os","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-os"},{"index":5510,"text":"      # If\nthe RuntimeClass admission controller is enabled, overhead must not be set in Pod create requests.\nThe RuntimeClass admission controller will reject Pod create requests which have the overhead already\nset. If RuntimeClass is configured and selected in the PodSpec, Overhead will be set to the value\ndefined in the corresponding RuntimeClass, otherwise it will remain unset and treated as zero.\nMore info: https://git.k8s.io/enhancements/keps/sig-node/688-pod-overhead/README.md","depth":2,"field":"overhead","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.overhead","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-overhead"},{"index":5529,"text":"      # <key>: <int-or-string> # intOrString","depth":3,"field":"<key>","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.overhead.<key>","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-overhead-key"},{"index":5530,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5530"},{"index":5531,"text":"    # PreemptionPolicy is the Policy for preempting pods with lower priority.","description":"PreemptionPolicy is the Policy for preempting pods with lower priority.","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.preemptionPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-preemptionpolicy"},{"index":5532,"text":"    # One of Never, PreemptLowerPriority. resourceClaimTemplateName: \"<string>\"","description":"ResourceClaimTemplateName is the name of a ResourceClaimTemplate\nobject in the same namespace as this pod.\n\nThe template will be used to create a new ResourceClaim, which will\nbe bound to this pod. When this pod is deleted, the ResourceClaim\nwill also be deleted. The pod name and resource name, along with a\ngenerated component, will be used to form a unique name for the\nResourceClaim, which will be recorded in pod.status.resourceClaimStatuses.\n\nThis field is immutable and no changes will be made to the\ncorresponding ResourceClaim by the control plane after creating the\nResourceClaim.\n\nExactly one of ResourceClaimName and ResourceClaimTemplateName must\nbe set.","depth":4,"field":"resourceClaimTemplateName","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.resourceClaims[].resourceClaimTemplateName","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-resourceclaims-resourceclaimtemplatename"},{"index":5597,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5597"},{"index":5598,"text":"    # Resources is the total amount of CPU and Memory resources required by all","description":"Resources is the total amount of CPU and Memory resources required by all","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.resources","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-resources"},{"index":5599,"text":"    # containers in the pod. It supports specifying Requests and Limits for\n\"cpu\", \"memory\" and \"hugepages-\" resource names only. ResourceClaims are not supported.\n\nThis field enables fine-grained control over resource allocation for the\nentire pod, allowing resource sharing among containers in a pod.\n\nThis is an alpha field and requires enabling the PodLevelResources feature\ngate.","depth":2,"field":"resources","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.resources","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-resources"},{"index":5609,"text":"      # Claims lists the names of resources, defined in spec.resourceClaims,","description":"Claims lists the names of resources, defined in spec.resourceClaims,","depth":3,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.resources.claims","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-resources-claims"},{"index":5610,"text":"      # that are used by this container.","description":"that are used by this container.","depth":3,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.resources.claims","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-resources-claims"},{"index":5611,"text":"      #","depth":3,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.resources.claims","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-resources-claims"},{"index":5612,"text":"      # This field depends on the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate.","description":"This field depends on the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate.","depth":3,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.resources.claims","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-resources-claims"},{"index":5613,"text":"      #","depth":3,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.resources.claims","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-resources-claims"},{"index":5614,"text":"      # This field is immutable. ContainerCreating state. Note that this field cannot be set when","description":"ContainerCreating state. Note that this field cannot be set when","depth":3,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.securityContext.seLinuxChangePolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-securitycontext-selinuxchangepolicy"},{"index":5765,"text":"      # spec.os.name is windows.","description":"spec.os.name is windows.","depth":3,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.securityContext.seLinuxChangePolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-securitycontext-selinuxchangepolicy"},{"index":5766,"text":"      # seLinuxChangePolicy: \"<string>\"","description":"seLinuxChangePolicy defines how the container's SELinux label is applied to all volumes used by the Pod.\nIt has no effect on nodes that do not support SELinux or to volumes does not support SELinux.\nValid values are \"MountOption\" and \"Recursive\".\n\n\"Recursive\" means relabeling of all files on all Pod volumes by the container runtime.\nThis may be slow for large volumes, but allows mixing privileged and unprivileged Pods sharing the same volume on the same node.\n\n\"MountOption\" mounts all eligible Pod volumes with `-o context` mount option.\nThis requires all Pods that share the same volume to use the same SELinux label.\nIt is not possible to share the same volume among privileged and unprivileged Pods.\nEligible volumes are in-tree FibreChannel and iSCSI volumes, and all CSI volumes\nwhose CSI driver announces SELinux support by setting spec.seLinuxMount: true in their\nCSIDriver instance. Other volumes are always re-labelled recursively.\n\"MountOption\" value is allowed only when SELinuxMount feature gate is enabled.\n\nIf not specified and SELinuxMount feature gate is enabled, \"MountOption\" is used.\nIf not specified and SELinuxMount feature gate is disabled, \"MountOption\" is used for ReadWriteOncePod volumes\nand \"Recursive\" for all other volumes.\n\nThis field affects only Pods that have SELinux label set, either in PodSecurityContext or in SecurityContext of all containers.\n\nAll Pods that use the same volume should use the same seLinuxChangePolicy, otherwise some pods can get stuck in ContainerCreating state.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","depth":3,"field":"seLinuxChangePolicy","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.securityContext.seLinuxChangePolicy","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-securitycontext-selinuxchangepolicy"},{"index":5767,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5767"},{"index":5768,"text":"      # The SELinux context to be applied to all containers. Optional: Default to false.","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.shareProcessNamespace","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-shareprocessnamespace"},{"index":5887,"text":"    # shareProcessNamespace: <boolean>","description":"Share a single process namespace between all of the containers in a pod.\nWhen this is set containers will be able to view and signal processes from other containers\nin the same pod, and the first process in each container will not be assigned PID 1.\nHostPID and ShareProcessNamespace cannot both be set.\nOptional: Default to false.","depth":2,"field":"shareProcessNamespace","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.shareProcessNamespace","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-shareprocessnamespace"},{"index":5888,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5888"},{"index":5889,"text":"    # If specified, the fully qualified Pod hostname will be","description":"If specified, the fully qualified Pod hostname will be","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.subdomain","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-subdomain"},{"index":5890,"text":"    # \"<hostname>.<subdomain>.<pod namespace>.svc.<cluster domain>\". If not","description":"\"<hostname>.<subdomain>.<pod namespace>.svc.<cluster domain>\". If not","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.subdomain","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-subdomain"},{"index":5891,"text":"    # specified, the pod will not have a domainname at all.","description":"specified, the pod will not have a domainname at all.","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.subdomain","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-subdomain"},{"index":5892,"text":"    # subdomain: \"<string>\"","description":"If specified, the fully qualified Pod hostname will be \"<hostname>.<subdomain>.<pod namespace>.svc.<cluster domain>\".\nIf not specified, the pod will not have a domainname at all.","depth":2,"field":"subdomain","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.subdomain","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-subdomain"},{"index":5893,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5893"},{"index":5894,"text":"    # Optional duration in seconds the pod needs to terminate gracefully. May be","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":5895,"text":"    # decreased in delete request. Value must be non-negative integer. The value","description":"decreased in delete request. Value must be non-negative integer. The value","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":5896,"text":"    # zero indicates stop immediately via the kill signal (no opportunity to","description":"zero indicates stop immediately via the kill signal (no opportunity to","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":5897,"text":"    # shut down). If this value is nil, the default grace period will be used","description":"shut down). If this value is nil, the default grace period will be used","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":5898,"text":"    # instead. The grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes","description":"instead. The grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":5899,"text":"    # running in the pod are sent a termination signal and the time when the","description":"running in the pod are sent a termination signal and the time when the","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":5900,"text":"    # processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal. Set this value longer","description":"processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal. Set this value longer","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":5901,"text":"    # than the expected cleanup time for your process. Defaults to 30 seconds.","description":"than the expected cleanup time for your process. Defaults to 30 seconds.","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":5902,"text":"    # terminationGracePeriodSeconds: <int64>","description":"Optional duration in seconds the pod needs to terminate gracefully. May be decreased in delete request.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nIf this value is nil, the default grace period will be used instead.\nThe grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes running in the pod are sent\na termination signal and the time when the processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal.\nSet this value longer than the expected cleanup time for your process.\nDefaults to 30 seconds.","depth":2,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":5903,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5903"},{"index":5904,"text":"    # If specified, the pod's tolerations.","description":"If specified, the pod's tolerations.","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations"},{"index":5905,"text":"    # tolerations: # listType: atomic","description":"If specified, the pod's tolerations.","depth":2,"field":"tolerations","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations"},{"index":5906,"text":"      # - # Effect indicates the taint effect to match. Empty means match all","description":"Effect indicates the taint effect to match. Empty means match all","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].effect","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-effect"},{"index":5907,"text":"        # taint effects. When specified, allowed values are NoSchedule,","description":"taint effects. When specified, allowed values are NoSchedule,","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].effect","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-effect"},{"index":5908,"text":"        # PreferNoSchedule and NoExecute.","description":"PreferNoSchedule and NoExecute.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].effect","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-effect"},{"index":5909,"text":"        # effect: \"<string>\"","description":"Effect indicates the taint effect to match. Empty means match all taint effects.\nWhen specified, allowed values are NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule and NoExecute.","depth":4,"field":"effect","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].effect","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-effect"},{"index":5910,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5910"},{"index":5911,"text":"        # Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].key","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-key"},{"index":5912,"text":"        # all taint keys. If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].key","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-key"},{"index":5913,"text":"        # combination means to match all values and all keys.","description":"combination means to match all values and all keys.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].key","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-key"},{"index":5914,"text":"        # key: \"<string>\"","description":"Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys.\nIf the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.","depth":4,"field":"key","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].key","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-key"},{"index":5915,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5915"},{"index":5916,"text":"        # Operator represents a key's relationship to the value. Defaults to Equal. Exists is equivalent to","description":"are Exists and Equal. Defaults to Equal. Exists is equivalent to","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].operator","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-operator"},{"index":5918,"text":"        # wildcard for value, so that a pod can tolerate all taints of a","description":"wildcard for value, so that a pod can tolerate all taints of a","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].operator","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-operator"},{"index":5919,"text":"        # particular category.","description":"particular category.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].operator","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-operator"},{"index":5920,"text":"        # operator: \"<string>\"","description":"Operator represents a key's relationship to the value.\nValid operators are Exists and Equal. Defaults to Equal.\nExists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a pod can\ntolerate all taints of a particular category.","depth":4,"field":"operator","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].operator","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-operator"},{"index":5921,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5921"},{"index":5922,"text":"        # TolerationSeconds represents the period of time the toleration (which","description":"TolerationSeconds represents the period of time the toleration (which","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].tolerationSeconds","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-tolerationseconds"},{"index":5923,"text":"        # must be of effect NoExecute, otherwise this field is ignored)","description":"must be of effect NoExecute, otherwise this field is ignored)","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].tolerationSeconds","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-tolerationseconds"},{"index":5924,"text":"        # tolerates the taint. By default, it is not set, which means tolerate","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].tolerationSeconds","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-tolerationseconds"},{"index":5925,"text":"        # the taint forever (do not evict). Zero and negative values will be","description":"the taint forever (do not evict). Zero and negative values will be","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].tolerationSeconds","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-tolerationseconds"},{"index":5926,"text":"        # treated as 0 (evict immediately) by the system.","description":"treated as 0 (evict immediately) by the system.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].tolerationSeconds","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-tolerationseconds"},{"index":5927,"text":"        # tolerationSeconds: <int64>","description":"TolerationSeconds represents the period of time the toleration (which must be\nof effect NoExecute, otherwise this field is ignored) tolerates the taint. Zero and\nnegative values will be treated as 0 (evict immediately) by the system.","depth":4,"field":"tolerationSeconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].tolerationSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-tolerationseconds"},{"index":5928,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5928"},{"index":5929,"text":"        # Value is the taint value the toleration matches to. If the operator is","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].value","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-value"},{"index":5930,"text":"        # Exists, the value should be empty, otherwise just a regular string.","description":"Exists, the value should be empty, otherwise just a regular string.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].value","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-value"},{"index":5931,"text":"        # value: \"<string>\"","description":"Value is the taint value the toleration matches to.\nIf the operator is Exists, the value should be empty, otherwise just a regular string.","depth":4,"field":"value","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.tolerations[].value","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-tolerations-value"},{"index":5932,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5932"},{"index":5933,"text":"    # TopologySpreadConstraints describes how a group of pods ought to spread","description":"TopologySpreadConstraints describes how a group of pods ought to spread","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints"},{"index":5934,"text":"    # across topology domains. All topologySpreadConstraints are ANDed.","description":"abides by the constraints. All topologySpreadConstraints are ANDed.","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints"},{"index":5936,"text":"    topologySpreadConstraints: # optional, listType: map, listMapKeys:","description":"TopologySpreadConstraints describes how a group of pods ought to spread across topology\ndomains. Scheduler will schedule pods in a way which abides by the constraints.\nAll topologySpreadConstraints are ANDed.","depth":2,"field":"topologySpreadConstraints","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints"},{"index":5937,"text":"                               # topologyKey, whenUnsatisfiable","description":"TopologySpreadConstraints describes how a group of pods ought to spread across topology\ndomains.
