When `whenUnsatisfiable=DoNotSchedule`, it is the maximum","description":"distributed. When `whenUnsatisfiable=DoNotSchedule`, it is the maximum","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":5940,"text":"        # permitted difference between the number of matching pods in the target","description":"permitted difference between the number of matching pods in the target","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":5941,"text":"        # topology and the global minimum. The global minimum is the minimum","description":"topology and the global minimum. The global minimum is the minimum","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":5942,"text":"        # number of matching pods in an eligible domain or zero if the number of","description":"number of matching pods in an eligible domain or zero if the number of","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":5943,"text":"        # eligible domains is less than MinDomains. For example, in a 3-zone","description":"eligible domains is less than MinDomains. For example, in a 3-zone","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":5944,"text":"        # cluster, MaxSkew is set to 1, and pods with the same labelSelector","description":"cluster, MaxSkew is set to 1, and pods with the same labelSelector","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":5945,"text":"        # spread as 2/2/1: In this case, the global minimum is 1. | zone1 |","description":"spread as 2/2/1: In this case, the global minimum is 1. | zone1 |","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":5946,"text":"        # zone2 | zone3 | | P P | P P | P | - if MaxSkew is 1, incoming pod can","description":"zone2 | zone3 | | P P | P P | P | - if MaxSkew is 1, incoming pod can","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":5947,"text":"        # only be scheduled to zone3 to become 2/2/2; scheduling it onto","description":"only be scheduled to zone3 to become 2/2/2; scheduling it onto","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":5948,"text":"        # zone1(zone2) would make the ActualSkew(3-1) on zone1(zone2) violate","description":"zone1(zone2) would make the ActualSkew(3-1) on zone1(zone2) violate","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":5949,"text":"        # MaxSkew(1). - if MaxSkew is 2, incoming pod can be scheduled onto any","description":"MaxSkew(1). - if MaxSkew is 2, incoming pod can be scheduled onto any","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":5950,"text":"        # zone. When `whenUnsatisfiable=ScheduleAnyway`, it is used to give","description":"zone. When `whenUnsatisfiable=ScheduleAnyway`, it is used to give","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":5951,"text":"        # higher precedence to topologies that satisfy it. It's a required","description":"higher precedence to topologies that satisfy it. It's a required","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":5952,"text":"        # field. Default value is 1 and 0 is not allowed.","description":"field. Default value is 1 and 0 is not allowed.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":5953,"text":"        maxSkew: <int32> # required","description":"MaxSkew describes the degree to which pods may be unevenly distributed.\nWhen `whenUnsatisfiable=DoNotSchedule`, it is the maximum permitted difference\nbetween the number of matching pods in the target topology and the global minimum.\nThe global minimum is the minimum number of matching pods in an eligible domain\nor zero if the number of eligible domains is less than MinDomains.\nFor example, in a 3-zone cluster, MaxSkew is set to 1, and pods with the same\nlabelSelector spread as 2/2/1:\nIn this case, the global minimum is 1.\n| zone1 | zone2 | zone3 |\n|  P P  |  P P  |   P   |\n- if MaxSkew is 1, incoming pod can only be scheduled to zone3 to become 2/2/2;\nscheduling it onto zone1(zone2) would make the ActualSkew(3-1) on zone1(zone2)\nviolate MaxSkew(1).\n- if MaxSkew is 2, incoming pod can be scheduled onto any zone.\nWhen `whenUnsatisfiable=ScheduleAnyway`, it is used to give higher precedence\nto topologies that satisfy it.\nIt's a required field. Default value is 1 and 0 is not allowed.","depth":4,"field":"maxSkew","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","code":true,"required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":5954,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5954"},{"index":5955,"text":"        # TopologyKey is the key of node labels. Nodes that have a label with","description":"TopologyKey is the key of node labels. Nodes that have a label with","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].topologyKey","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-topologykey"},{"index":5956,"text":"        # this key and identical values are considered to be in the same","description":"this key and identical values are considered to be in the same","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].topologyKey","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-topologykey"},{"index":5957,"text":"        # topology. We consider each <key, value> as a \"bucket\", and try to put","description":"topology. We consider each <key, value> as a \"bucket\", and try to put","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].topologyKey","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-topologykey"},{"index":5958,"text":"        # balanced number of pods into each bucket. We define a domain as a","description":"balanced number of pods into each bucket. We define a domain as a","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].topologyKey","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-topologykey"},{"index":5959,"text":"        # particular instance of a topology. Also, we define an eligible domain","description":"particular instance of a topology. Also, we define an eligible domain","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].topologyKey","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-topologykey"},{"index":5960,"text":"        # as a domain whose nodes meet the requirements of nodeAffinityPolicy","description":"as a domain whose nodes meet the requirements of nodeAffinityPolicy","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].topologyKey","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-topologykey"},{"index":5961,"text":"        # and nodeTaintsPolicy. e.g. If TopologyKey is \"kubernetes.io/hostname\",","description":"and nodeTaintsPolicy. e.g. If TopologyKey is \"kubernetes.io/hostname\",","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].topologyKey","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-topologykey"},{"index":5962,"text":"        # each Node is a domain of that topology. And, if TopologyKey is","description":"each Node is a domain of that topology. And, if TopologyKey is","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].topologyKey","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-topologykey"},{"index":5963,"text":"        # \"topology.kubernetes.io/zone\", each zone is a domain of that topology.","description":"\"topology.kubernetes.io/zone\", each zone is a domain of that topology.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].topologyKey","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-topologykey"},{"index":5964,"text":"        # It's a required field.","description":"It's a required field.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].topologyKey","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-topologykey"},{"index":5965,"text":"        topologyKey: \"<string>\" # required","description":"TopologyKey is the key of node labels. Nodes that have a label with this key\nand identical values are considered to be in the same topology.\nWe consider each <key, value> as a \"bucket\", and try to put balanced number\nof pods into each bucket.\nWe define a domain as a particular instance of a topology.\nAlso, we define an eligible domain as a domain whose nodes meet the requirements of\nnodeAffinityPolicy and nodeTaintsPolicy.\ne.g. If TopologyKey is \"kubernetes.io/hostname\", each Node is a domain of that topology.\nAnd, if TopologyKey is \"topology.kubernetes.io/zone\", each zone is a domain of that topology.\nIt's a required field.","depth":4,"field":"topologyKey","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].topologyKey","code":true,"required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-topologykey"},{"index":5966,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5966"},{"index":5967,"text":"        # WhenUnsatisfiable indicates how to deal with a pod if it doesn't","description":"WhenUnsatisfiable indicates how to deal with a pod if it doesn't","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].whenUnsatisfiable","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-whenunsatisfiable"},{"index":5968,"text":"        # satisfy the spread constraint. - It's a required field.","description":"*more* imbalanced. It's a required field.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].whenUnsatisfiable","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-whenunsatisfiable"},{"index":5981,"text":"        whenUnsatisfiable: \"<string>\" # required","description":"WhenUnsatisfiable indicates how to deal with a pod if it doesn't satisfy\nthe spread constraint.\n- DoNotSchedule (default) tells the scheduler not to schedule it.\n- ScheduleAnyway tells the scheduler to schedule the pod in any location,\n  but giving higher precedence to topologies that would help reduce the\n  skew.\nA constraint is considered \"Unsatisfiable\" for an incoming pod\nif and only if every possible node assignment for that pod would violate\n\"MaxSkew\" on some topology.\nFor example, in a 3-zone cluster, MaxSkew is set to 1, and pods with the same\nlabelSelector spread as 3/1/1:\n| zone1 | zone2 | zone3 |\n| P P P |   P   |   P   |\nIf WhenUnsatisfiable is set to DoNotSchedule, incoming pod can only be scheduled\nto zone2(zone3) to become 3/2/1(3/1/2) as ActualSkew(2-1) on zone2(zone3) satisfies\nMaxSkew(1). In other words, the cluster can still be imbalanced, but scheduler\nwon't make it *more* imbalanced.\nIt's a required field.","depth":4,"field":"whenUnsatisfiable","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].whenUnsatisfiable","code":true,"required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-whenunsatisfiable"},{"index":5982,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5982"},{"index":5983,"text":"        # LabelSelector is used to find matching pods. The same key is forbidden to exist in both MatchLabelKeys and LabelSelector.\nMatchLabelKeys cannot be set when LabelSelector isn't set.\nKeys that don't exist in the incoming pod labels will\nbe ignored. When value is not nil, WhenUnsatisfiable must","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].minDomains","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-mindomains"},{"index":6037,"text":"        # be DoNotSchedule.","description":"be DoNotSchedule.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].minDomains","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-mindomains"},{"index":6038,"text":"        #","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].minDomains","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-mindomains"},{"index":6039,"text":"        # For example, in a 3-zone cluster, MaxSkew is set to 2, MinDomains is","description":"For example, in a 3-zone cluster, MaxSkew is set to 2, MinDomains is","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].minDomains","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-mindomains"},{"index":6040,"text":"        # set to 5 and pods with the same labelSelector spread as 2/2/2: | zone1","description":"set to 5 and pods with the same labelSelector spread as 2/2/2: | zone1","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].minDomains","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-mindomains"},{"index":6041,"text":"        # | zone2 | zone3 | | P P | P P | P P | The number of domains is less","description":"| zone2 | zone3 | | P P | P P | P P | The number of domains is less","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].minDomains","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-mindomains"},{"index":6042,"text":"        # than 5(MinDomains), so \"global minimum\" is treated as 0. In this","description":"than 5(MinDomains), so \"global minimum\" is treated as 0. In this","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].minDomains","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-mindomains"},{"index":6043,"text":"        # situation, new pod with the same labelSelector cannot be scheduled,","description":"situation, new pod with the same labelSelector cannot be scheduled,","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].minDomains","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-mindomains"},{"index":6044,"text":"        # because computed skew will be 3(3 - 0) if new Pod is scheduled to any","description":"because computed skew will be 3(3 - 0) if new Pod is scheduled to any","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].minDomains","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-mindomains"},{"index":6045,"text":"        # of the three zones, it will violate MaxSkew.","description":"of the three zones, it will violate MaxSkew.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].minDomains","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-mindomains"},{"index":6046,"text":"        # minDomains: <int32>","description":"MinDomains indicates a minimum number of eligible domains.\nWhen the number of eligible domains with matching topology keys is less than minDomains,\nPod Topology Spread treats \"global minimum\" as 0, and then the calculation of Skew is performed.\nAnd when the number of eligible domains with matching topology keys equals or greater than minDomains,\nthis value has no effect on scheduling.\nAs a result, when the number of eligible domains is less than minDomains,\nscheduler won't schedule more than maxSkew Pods to those domains.\nIf value is nil, the constraint behaves as if MinDomains is equal to 1.\nValid values are integers greater than 0.\nWhen value is not nil, WhenUnsatisfiable must be DoNotSchedule.\n\nFor example, in a 3-zone cluster, MaxSkew is set to 2, MinDomains is set to 5 and pods with the same\nlabelSelector spread as 2/2/2:\n| zone1 | zone2 | zone3 |\n|  P P  |  P P  |  P P  |\nThe number of domains is less than 5(MinDomains), so \"global minimum\" is treated as 0.\nIn this situation, new pod with the same labelSelector cannot be scheduled,\nbecause computed skew will be 3(3 - 0) if new Pod is scheduled to any of the three zones,\nit will violate MaxSkew.","depth":4,"field":"minDomains","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].minDomains","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-mindomains"},{"index":6047,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-6047"},{"index":6048,"text":"        # NodeAffinityPolicy indicates how we will treat Pod's","description":"NodeAffinityPolicy indicates how we will treat Pod's","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeAffinityPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodeaffinitypolicy"},{"index":6049,"text":"        # nodeAffinity/nodeSelector when calculating pod topology spread skew.","description":"nodeAffinity/nodeSelector when calculating pod topology spread skew.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeAffinityPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodeaffinitypolicy"},{"index":6050,"text":"        # Options are: - Honor: only nodes matching nodeAffinity/nodeSelector","description":"Options are: - Honor: only nodes matching nodeAffinity/nodeSelector","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeAffinityPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodeaffinitypolicy"},{"index":6051,"text":"        # are included in the calculations. - Ignore: nodeAffinity/nodeSelector","description":"are included in the calculations. - Ignore: nodeAffinity/nodeSelector","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeAffinityPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodeaffinitypolicy"},{"index":6052,"text":"        # are ignored. All nodes are included in the calculations.","description":"are ignored. All nodes are included in the calculations.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeAffinityPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodeaffinitypolicy"},{"index":6053,"text":"        #","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeAffinityPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodeaffinitypolicy"},{"index":6054,"text":"        # If this value is nil, the behavior is equivalent to the Honor policy.","description":"If this value is nil, the behavior is equivalent to the Honor policy.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeAffinityPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodeaffinitypolicy"},{"index":6055,"text":"        # nodeAffinityPolicy: \"<string>\"","description":"NodeAffinityPolicy indicates how we will treat Pod's nodeAffinity/nodeSelector\nwhen calculating pod topology spread skew. Options are:\n- Honor: only nodes matching nodeAffinity/nodeSelector are included in the calculations.\n- Ignore: nodeAffinity/nodeSelector are ignored. All nodes are included in the calculations.\n\nIf this value is nil, the behavior is equivalent to the Honor policy.","depth":4,"field":"nodeAffinityPolicy","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeAffinityPolicy","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodeaffinitypolicy"},{"index":6056,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-6056"},{"index":6057,"text":"        # NodeTaintsPolicy indicates how we will treat node taints when","description":"NodeTaintsPolicy indicates how we will treat node taints when","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeTaintsPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodetaintspolicy"},{"index":6058,"text":"        # calculating pod topology spread skew. Options are: - Honor: nodes","description":"calculating pod topology spread skew. Options are: - Honor: nodes","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeTaintsPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodetaintspolicy"},{"index":6059,"text":"        # without taints, along with tainted nodes for which the incoming pod","description":"without taints, along with tainted nodes for which the incoming pod","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeTaintsPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodetaintspolicy"},{"index":6060,"text":"        # has a toleration, are included. - Ignore: node taints are ignored. All","description":"has a toleration, are included. - Ignore: node taints are ignored. All","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeTaintsPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodetaintspolicy"},{"index":6061,"text":"        # nodes are included.","description":"nodes are included.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeTaintsPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodetaintspolicy"},{"index":6062,"text":"        #","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeTaintsPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodetaintspolicy"},{"index":6063,"text":"        # If this value is nil, the behavior is equivalent to the Ignore policy.","description":"If this value is nil, the behavior is equivalent to the Ignore policy.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeTaintsPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodetaintspolicy"},{"index":6064,"text":"        # nodeTaintsPolicy: \"<string>\"","description":"NodeTaintsPolicy indicates how we will treat node taints when calculating\npod topology spread skew. Options are:\n- Honor: nodes without taints, along with tainted nodes for which the incoming pod\nhas a toleration, are included.\n- Ignore: node taints are ignored. All nodes are included.\n\nIf this value is nil, the behavior is equivalent to the Ignore policy.","depth":4,"field":"nodeTaintsPolicy","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeTaintsPolicy","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodetaintspolicy"},{"index":6065,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-6065"},{"index":6066,"text":"    # List of volumes that can be mounted by containers belonging to the pod.","description":"List of volumes that can be mounted by containers belonging to the pod.","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.volumes","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-volumes"},{"index":6067,"text":"    # More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes","description":"More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes","depth":2,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.volumes","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-volumes"},{"index":6068,"text":"    volumes: # optional, listType: map, listMapKeys: name","description":"List of volumes that can be mounted by containers belonging to the pod.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes","depth":2,"field":"volumes","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.volumes","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-volumes"},{"index":6069,"text":"      - # name of the volume. optional","description":"ephemeral represents a volume that is handled by a cluster storage driver.\nThe volume's lifecycle is tied to the pod that defines it - it will be created before the pod starts,\nand deleted when the pod is removed.\n\nUse this if:\na) the volume is only needed while the pod runs,\nb) features of normal volumes like restoring from snapshot or capacity\n   tracking are needed,\nc) the storage driver is specified through a storage class, and\nd) the storage driver supports dynamic volume provisioning through\n   a PersistentVolumeClaim (see EphemeralVolumeSource for more\n   information on the connection between this volume type\n   and PersistentVolumeClaim).\n\nUse PersistentVolumeClaim or one of the vendor-specific\nAPIs for volumes that persist for longer than the lifecycle\nof an individual pod.\n\nUse CSI for light-weight local ephemeral volumes if the CSI driver is meant to\nbe used that way - see the documentation of the driver for\nmore information.\n\nA pod can use both types of ephemeral volumes and\npersistent volumes at the same time.","depth":4,"field":"ephemeral","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.volumes[].ephemeral","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-volumes-ephemeral"},{"index":6399,"text":"          # optional","description":"Will be used to create a stand-alone PVC to provision the volume.\nThe pod in which this EphemeralVolumeSource is embedded will be the\nowner of the PVC, i.e. the PVC will be deleted together with the\npod.  The name of the PVC will be `<pod name>-<volume name>` where\n`<volume name>` is the name from the `PodSpec.Volumes` array\nentry. Pod validation will reject the pod if the concatenated name\nis not valid for a PVC (for example, too long).\n\nAn existing PVC with that name that is not owned by the pod\nwill *not* be used for the pod to avoid using an unrelated\nvolume by mistake. Starting the pod is then blocked until\nthe unrelated PVC is removed. If such a pre-created PVC is\nmeant to be used by the pod, the PVC has to updated with an\nowner reference to the pod once the pod exists. Normally\nthis should not be necessary, but it may be useful when\nmanually reconstructing a broken cluster.\n\nThis field is read-only and no changes will be made by Kubernetes\nto the PVC after it has been created.\n\nRequired, must not be nil.","depth":5,"field":"volumeClaimTemplate","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate"},{"index":6420,"text":"            # The specification for the PersistentVolumeClaim. optional, mapType: atomic","description":"dataSource field can be used to specify either:\n* An existing VolumeSnapshot object (snapshot.storage.k8s.io/VolumeSnapshot)\n* An existing PVC (PersistentVolumeClaim)\nIf the provisioner or an external controller can support the specified data source,\nit will create a new volume based on the contents of the specified data source.\nWhen the AnyVolumeDataSource feature gate is enabled, dataSource contents will be copied to dataSourceRef,\nand dataSourceRef contents will be copied to dataSource when dataSourceRef.namespace is not specified.\nIf the namespace is specified, then dataSourceRef will not be copied to dataSource.","depth":7,"field":"dataSource","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSource","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate-spec-datasource"},{"index":6442,"text":"                # This may be any object from a non-empty API group (non\ncore object) or a PersistentVolumeClaim object.\nWhen this field is specified, volume binding will only succeed if the type of\nthe specified object matches some installed volume populator or dynamic\nprovisioner.\nThis field will replace the functionality of the dataSource field and as such\nif both fields are non-empty, they must have the same value. For backwards\ncompatibility, when namespace isn't specified in dataSourceRef,\nboth fields (dataSource and dataSourceRef) will be set to the same\nvalue automatically if one of them is empty and the other is non-empty.\nWhen namespace is specified in dataSourceRef,\ndataSource isn't set to the same value and must be empty.\nThere are three important differences between dataSource and dataSourceRef:\n* While dataSource only allows two specific types of objects, dataSourceRef\n  allows any non-core object, as well as PersistentVolumeClaim objects.\n* While dataSource ignores disallowed values (dropping them), dataSourceRef\n  preserves all values, and generates an error if a disallowed value is\n  specified.\n* While dataSource only allows local objects, dataSourceRef allows objects\n  in any namespaces.\n(Beta) Using this field requires the AnyVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.\n(Alpha) Using the namespace field of dataSourceRef requires the CrossNamespaceVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.","depth":7,"field":"dataSourceRef","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate-spec-datasourceref"},{"index":6481,"text":"                # The field","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.volumes[].image","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-volumes-image"},{"index":6777,"text":"        # spec.securityContext.fsGroupChangePolicy has no effect on this volume","description":"spec.securityContext.fsGroupChangePolicy has no effect on this volume","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.volumes[].image","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-volumes-image"},{"index":6778,"text":"        # type.","description":"type.","depth":4,"path":"spec.extraPodSpec.volumes[].image","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-volumes-image"},{"index":6779,"text":"        # image:","description":"image represents an OCI object (a container image or artifact) pulled and mounted on the kubelet's host machine.\nThe volume is resolved at pod startup depending on which PullPolicy value is provided:\n\n- Always: the kubelet always attempts to pull the reference. Container creation will fail If the pull fails.\n- Never: the kubelet never pulls the reference and only uses a local image or artifact. Container creation will fail if the reference isn't present.\n- IfNotPresent: the kubelet pulls if the reference isn't already present on disk. Container creation will fail if the reference isn't present and the pull fails.\n\nThe volume gets re-resolved if the pod gets deleted and recreated, which means that new remote content will become available on pod recreation.\nA failure to resolve or pull the image during pod startup will block containers from starting and may add significant latency. Failures will be retried using normal volume backoff and will be reported on the pod reason and message.\nThe types of objects that may be mounted by this volume are defined by the container runtime implementation on a host machine and at minimum must include all valid types supported by the container image field.\nThe OCI object gets mounted in a single directory (spec.containers[*].volumeMounts.mountPath) by merging the manifest layers in the same way as for container images.\nThe volume will be mounted read-only (ro) and non-executable files (noexec).\nSub path mounts for containers are not supported (spec.containers[*].volumeMounts.subpath) before 1.33.\nThe field spec.securityContext.fsGroupChangePolicy has no effect on this volume type.","depth":4,"field":"image","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.volumes[].image","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-volumes-image"},{"index":6780,"text":"          # Policy for pulling OCI objects. Once the signer approves the\nrequest and issues a certificate chain, Kubelet writes the key and\ncertificate chain to the pod filesystem.  The pod does not start until\ncertificates have been issued for each podCertificate projected volume\nsource in its spec.\n\nKubelet will begin trying to rotate the certificate at the time indicated\nby the signer using the PodCertificateRequest.Status.BeginRefreshAt\ntimestamp.\n\nKubelet can write a single file, indicated by the credentialBundlePath\nfield, or separate files, indicated by the keyPath and\ncertificateChainPath fields.\n\nThe credential bundle is a single file in PEM format.  The first PEM\nentry is the private key (in PKCS#8 format), and the remaining PEM\nentries are the certificate chain issued by the signer (typically,\nsigners will return their certificate chain in leaf-to-root order).\n\nPrefer using the credential bundle format, since your application code\ncan read it atomically.  If you use keyPath and certificateChainPath,\nyour application must make two separate file reads. If these coincide\nwith a certificate rotation, it is possible that the private key and leaf\ncertificate you read may not correspond to each other.  Your application\nwill need to check for this condition, and re-read until they are\nconsistent.\n\nThe named signer controls chooses the format of the certificate it\nissues; consult the signer implementation's documentation to learn how to\nuse the certificates it issues.","depth":7,"field":"podCertificate","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.volumes[].projected.sources[].podCertificate","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-volumes-projected-sources-podcertificate"},{"index":7127,"text":"                # The maximum allowable\nvalue is 7862400 (91 days).\n\nThe signer implementation is then free to issue a certificate with any\nlifetime *shorter* than MaxExpirationSeconds, but no shorter than 3600\nseconds (1 hour). For interPod mode: the operator creates a dedicated GMS","depth":1,"path":"spec.failover","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-failover"},{"index":7487,"text":"  # weight server pod and multiple engine pods per rank that share GPUs via DRA","description":"weight server pod and multiple engine pods per rank that share GPUs via DRA","depth":1,"path":"spec.failover","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-failover"},{"index":7488,"text":"  # resource claims.","description":"resource claims.","depth":1,"path":"spec.failover","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-failover"},{"index":7489,"text":"  failover: # optional","description":"Failover configures GMS (GPU Memory Service) failover for this service.\nFor intraPod mode: the main container is cloned into two engine containers (active + standby).\nFor interPod mode: the operator creates a dedicated GMS weight server pod and\nmultiple engine pods per rank that share GPUs via DRA resource claims.","depth":1,"field":"failover","path":"spec.failover","code":true,"foldable":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-failover"},{"index":7490,"text":"    # Mode selects the failover deployment topology. intraPod: engine containers","description":"Mode selects the failover deployment topology. intraPod: engine containers","depth":2,"path":"spec.failover.mode","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-failover-mode"},{"index":7494,"text":"    # run within the same pod (requires gpuMemoryService.enabled). interPod: a","description":"run within the same pod (requires gpuMemoryService.enabled). interPod: a","depth":2,"path":"spec.failover.mode","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-failover-mode"},{"index":7495,"text":"    # dedicated GMS weight server pod + engine pods per rank (requires Grove).","description":"dedicated GMS weight server pod + engine pods per rank (requires Grove).","depth":2,"path":"spec.failover.mode","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-failover-mode"},{"index":7496,"text":"    # mode: \"intraPod\" # default, enum: \"interPod\"","description":"Mode selects the failover deployment topology.\nintraPod: engine containers run within the same pod (requires gpuMemoryService.enabled).\ninterPod: a dedicated GMS weight server pod + engine pods per rank (requires Grove).","depth":2,"field":"mode","path":"spec.failover.mode","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-failover-mode"},{"index":7497,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-7497"},{"index":7498,"text":"    # NumShadows is the number of shadow (standby) engine pods per rank. Total","description":"NumShadows is the number of shadow (standby) engine pods per rank. Total","depth":2,"path":"spec.failover.numShadows","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-failover-numshadows"},{"index":7499,"text":"    # engine pods per rank = NumShadows + 1 (1 primary + NumShadows shadows).","description":"engine pods per rank = NumShadows + 1 (1 primary + NumShadows shadows).","depth":2,"path":"spec.failover.numShadows","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-failover-numshadows"},{"index":7500,"text":"    #","depth":2,"path":"spec.failover.numShadows","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-failover-numshadows"},{"index":7501,"text":"    # NumShadows is only meaningful for mode=interPod; intraPod uses a fixed 1","description":"NumShadows is only meaningful for mode=interPod; intraPod uses a fixed 1","depth":2,"path":"spec.failover.numShadows","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-failover-numshadows"},{"index":7502,"text":"    # primary + 1 shadow sidecar layout and any value other than 1 is rejected","description":"primary + 1 shadow sidecar layout and any value other than 1 is rejected","depth":2,"path":"spec.failover.numShadows","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-failover-numshadows"},{"index":7503,"text":"    # at admission time.","description":"at admission time.","depth":2,"path":"spec.failover.numShadows","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-failover-numshadows"},{"index":7504,"text":"    # numShadows: 1 # default, minimum: 1","description":"NumShadows is the number of shadow (standby) engine pods per rank.\nTotal engine pods per rank = NumShadows + 1 (1 primary + NumShadows shadows).\n\nNumShadows is only meaningful for mode=interPod; (GAIE)","depth":1,"path":"spec.frontendSidecar","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-frontendsidecar"},{"index":7511,"text":"  frontendSidecar: # optional","description":"FrontendSidecar configures an auto-generated frontend sidecar container.\nWhen specified, the operator injects a fully configured frontend container\nwith all standard Dynamo environment variables, health probes, and ports.\nThis eliminates the need to manually specify these in extraPodSpec.containers. (GAIE)","depth":1,"field":"frontendSidecar","path":"spec.frontendSidecar","code":true,"foldable":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-frontendsidecar"},{"index":7512,"text":"    # Image is the container image for the frontend sidecar.","description":"Image is the container image for the frontend sidecar.","depth":2,"path":"spec.frontendSidecar.image","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-frontendsidecar-image"},{"index":7513,"text":"    image: \"<string>\" # required","description":"Image is the container image for the frontend sidecar.","depth":2,"field":"image","path":"spec.frontendSidecar.image","code":true,"required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-frontendsidecar-image"},{"index":7514,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-7514"},{"index":7515,"text":"    # Args overrides the default frontend arguments. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":2,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.livenessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-livenessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":7800,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-7800"},{"index":7801,"text":"    # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":2,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.readinessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-readinessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":7910,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-7910"},{"index":7911,"text":"    # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. component\n(such as Pod, Service, and Ingress when applicable)."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-annotations-key","path":"spec.annotations.<key>","type":"string","required":false},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-autoscaling","path":"spec.autoscaling","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Deprecated: This field is deprecated and ignored. Use DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapter\nwith HPA, KEDA, or Planner for autoscaling instead. See docs/kubernetes/autoscaling.md\nfor migration guidance. This field will be removed in a future API version."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-autoscaling-behavior","path":"spec.autoscaling.behavior","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Deprecated: This field is ignored."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-autoscaling-behavior-scaledown","path":"spec.autoscaling.behavior.scaleDown","type":"object","required":false,"description":"scaleDown is scaling policy for scaling Down.\nIf not set, the default value is to allow to scale down to minReplicas pods, with a\n300 second stabilization window (i.e., the highest recommendation for\nthe last 300sec is used)."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-autoscaling-behavior-scaledown-policies","path":"spec.autoscaling.behavior.scaleDown.policies","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"policies is a list of potential scaling polices which can be used during scaling.\nIf not set, use the default values:\n- For scale up: allow doubling the number of pods, or an absolute change of 4 pods in a 15s window.\n- For scale down: allow all pods to be removed in a 15s window.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-autoscaling-behavior-scaledown-policies-periodseconds","path":"spec.autoscaling.behavior.scaleDown.policies[].periodSeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":true,"description":"periodSeconds specifies the window of time for which the policy should hold true.\nPeriodSeconds must be greater than zero and less than or equal to 1800 (30 min).","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-autoscaling-behavior-scaledown-policies-type","path":"spec.autoscaling.behavior.scaleDown.policies[].type","type":"string","required":true,"description":"type is used to specify the scaling policy."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-autoscaling-behavior-scaledown-policies-value","path":"spec.autoscaling.behavior.scaleDown.policies[].value","type":"integer/int32","required":true,"description":"value contains the amount of change which is permitted by the policy.\nIt must be greater than zero","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-autoscaling-behavior-scaledown-selectpolicy","path":"spec.autoscaling.behavior.scaleDown.selectPolicy","type":"string","required":false,"description":"selectPolicy is used to specify which policy should be used.\nIf not set, the default value Max is used."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-autoscaling-behavior-scaledown-stabilizationwindowseconds","path":"spec.autoscaling.behavior.scaleDown.stabilizationWindowSeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"stabilizationWindowSeconds is the number of seconds for which past recommendations should be\nconsidered while scaling up or scaling down.\nStabilizationWindowSeconds must be greater than or equal to zero and less than or equal to 3600 (one hour).\nIf not set, use the default values:\n- For scale up: 0 (i.e. no stabilization is done).\n- For scale down: 300 (i.e. the stabilization window is 300 seconds long).","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-autoscaling-behavior-scaledown-tolerance","path":"spec.autoscaling.behavior.scaleDown.tolerance","type":"int-or-string","required":false,"description":"tolerance is the tolerance on the ratio between the current and desired\nmetric value under which no updates are made to the desired number of\nreplicas (e.g. 0.01 for 1%). Must be greater than or equal to zero. If not\nset, the default cluster-wide tolerance is applied (by default 10%).\n\nFor example, if autoscaling is configured with a memory consumption target of 100Mi,\nand scale-down and scale-up tolerances of 5% and 1% respectively, scaling will be\ntriggered when the actual consumption falls below 95Mi or exceeds 101Mi.\n\nThis is an alpha field and requires enabling the HPAConfigurableTolerance\nfeature gate.","metadata":["pattern: ^(\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))(([KMGTPE]i)|[numkMGTPE]|([eE](\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))))?$","x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-autoscaling-behavior-scaleup","path":"spec.autoscaling.behavior.scaleUp","type":"object","required":false,"description":"scaleUp is scaling policy for scaling Up.\nIf not set, the default value is the higher of:\n  * increase no more than 4 pods per 60 seconds\n  * double the number of pods per 60 seconds\nNo stabilization is used."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-autoscaling-behavior-scaleup-policies","path":"spec.autoscaling.behavior.scaleUp.policies","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"policies is a list of potential scaling polices which can be used during scaling.\nIf not set, use the default values:\n- For scale up: allow doubling the number of pods, or an absolute change of 4 pods in a 15s window.\n- For scale down: allow all pods to be removed in a 15s window.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-autoscaling-behavior-scaleup-policies-periodseconds","path":"spec.autoscaling.behavior.scaleUp.policies[].periodSeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":true,"description":"periodSeconds specifies the window of time for which the policy should hold true.\nPeriodSeconds must be greater than zero and less than or equal to 1800 (30 min).","metadata":["format: If not\nset, the default cluster-wide tolerance is applied (by default 10%).\n\nFor example, if autoscaling is configured with a memory consumption target of 100Mi,\nand scale-down and scale-up tolerances of 5% and 1% respectively, scaling will be\ntriggered when the actual consumption falls below 95Mi or exceeds 101Mi.\n\nThis is an alpha field and requires enabling the HPAConfigurableTolerance\nfeature gate.","metadata":["pattern: ^(\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))(([KMGTPE]i)|[numkMGTPE]|([eE](\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))))?$","x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-autoscaling-enabled","path":"spec.autoscaling.enabled","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Deprecated: This field is ignored."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-autoscaling-maxreplicas","path":"spec.autoscaling.maxReplicas","type":"integer","required":false,"description":"Deprecated: This field is ignored."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-autoscaling-metrics","path":"spec.autoscaling.metrics","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"Deprecated: This field is ignored."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-autoscaling-metrics-containerresource","path":"spec.autoscaling.metrics[].containerResource","type":"object","required":false,"description":"containerResource refers to a resource metric (such as those specified in\nrequests and limits) known to Kubernetes describing a single container in\neach pod of the current scale target (e.g. CPU or memory). It also contains a MainContainer (standard k8s Container) field\nthat allows overriding the main container configuration."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-activedeadlineseconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.activeDeadlineSeconds","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"Optional duration in seconds the pod may be active on the node relative to\nStartTime before the system will actively try to mark it failed and kill associated containers.\nValue must be a positive integer.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity","type":"object","required":false,"description":"If specified, the pod's scheduling constraints"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-nodeaffinity","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.nodeAffinity","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Describes node affinity scheduling rules for the pod."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-nodeaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. The node that is\nmost preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.\nfor each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource\nrequest, requiredDuringScheduling affinity expressions, etc.),\ncompute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and adding\n\"weight\" to the sum if the node matches the corresponding matchExpressions; the\nnode(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-nodeaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-preference","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].preference","type":"object","required":true,"description":"A node selector term, associated with the corresponding weight.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-map-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-nodeaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-preference-matchexpressions","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].preference.matchExpressions","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"A list of node selector requirements by node's labels.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-nodeaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-preference-matchexpressions-key","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].preference.matchExpressions[].key","type":"string","required":true,"description":"The label key that the selector applies to."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-nodeaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-preference-matchexpressions-operator","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].preference.matchExpressions[].operator","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Represents a key's relationship to a set of values.\nValid operators are In, NotIn, Exists, DoesNotExist. The requirements are ANDed."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-podaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm-labelselector-matchlabels-key","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.podAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm.labelSelector.matchLabels.<key>","type":"string","required":false},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-podaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm-matchlabelkeys","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.podAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm.matchLabelKeys","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"MatchLabelKeys is a set of pod label keys to select which pods will\nbe taken into consideration. The keys are used to lookup values from the\nincoming pod labels, those key-value labels are merged with `labelSelector` as `key in (value)`\nto select the group of existing pods which pods will be taken into consideration\nfor the incoming pod's pod (anti) affinity. Keys that don't exist in the incoming\npod labels will be ignored. The default value is empty.\nThe same key is forbidden to exist in both matchLabelKeys and labelSelector.\nAlso, matchLabelKeys cannot be set when labelSelector isn't set.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-podaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm-matchlabelkeys","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.podAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm.matchLabelKeys[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-podaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm-mismatchlabelkeys","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.podAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm.mismatchLabelKeys","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"MismatchLabelKeys is a set of pod label keys to select which pods will\nbe taken into consideration. int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-podaffinity-requiredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.podAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"If the affinity requirements specified by this field are not met at\nscheduling time, the pod will not be scheduled onto the node.\nIf the affinity requirements specified by this field cease to be met\nat some point during pod execution (e.g. due to a pod label update), the\nsystem may or may not try to eventually evict the pod from its node.\nWhen there are multiple elements, the lists of The keys are used to lookup values from the\nincoming pod labels, those key-value labels are merged with `labelSelector` as `key in (value)`\nto select the group of existing pods which pods will be taken into consideration\nfor the incoming pod's pod (anti) affinity. Keys that don't exist in the incoming\npod labels will be ignored. scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe anti-affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. The keys are used to lookup values from the\nincoming pod labels, those key-value labels are merged with `labelSelector` as `key in (value)`\nto select the group of existing pods which pods will be taken into consideration\nfor the incoming pod's pod (anti) affinity. Keys that don't exist in the incoming\npod labels will be ignored. The default value is empty.\nThe same key is forbidden to exist in both matchLabelKeys and labelSelector.\nAlso, matchLabelKeys cannot be set when labelSelector isn't set.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-podantiaffinity-requiredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-matchlabelkeys","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].matchLabelKeys[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-affinity-podantiaffinity-requiredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-mismatchlabelkeys","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].mismatchLabelKeys","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"MismatchLabelKeys is a set of pod label keys to select which pods will\nbe taken into consideration. Regardless of the outcome of the handler, the\ncontainer will eventually terminate within the Pod's termination grace\nperiod (unless delayed by finalizers). Must be 1 for liveness and startup. Minimum value is 1.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-livenessprobe-tcpsocket","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].livenessProbe.tcpSocket","type":"object","required":false,"description":"TCPSocket specifies a connection to a TCP port.","metadata":["requiredFields: port"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-livenessprobe-tcpsocket-host","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].livenessProbe.tcpSocket.host","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Optional: Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-livenessprobe-tcpsocket-port","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].livenessProbe.tcpSocket.port","type":"int-or-string","required":true,"description":"Number or name of the port to access on the container.\nNumber must be in the range 1 to 65535.\nName must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-livenessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].livenessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"Optional duration in seconds the pod needs to terminate gracefully upon probe failure.\nThe grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes running in the pod are sent\na termination signal and the time when the processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal.\nSet this value longer than the expected cleanup time for your process.\nIf this value is nil, the pod's terminationGracePeriodSeconds will be used. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-livenessprobe-timeoutseconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].livenessProbe.timeoutSeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Number of seconds after which the probe times out.\nDefaults to 1 second. Minimum value is 1.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-name","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Name of the container specified as a DNS_LABEL.\nEach container in a pod must have a unique name (DNS_LABEL).\nCannot be updated."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-ports","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].ports","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"List of ports to expose from the container. Not specifying a port here\nDOES NOT prevent that port from being exposed. Any port which is\nlistening on the default \"0.0.0.0\" address inside a container will be\naccessible from the network.\nModifying this array with strategic merge patch may corrupt the data.\nFor more information See https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/108255.\nCannot be updated.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: map","x-kubernetes-list-map-keys: containerPort, protocol"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-ports-containerport","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].ports[].containerPort","type":"integer/int32","required":true,"description":"Number of port to expose on the pod's IP address.\nThis must be a valid port number, 0 < x < 65536.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-ports-hostip","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].ports[].hostIP","type":"string","required":false,"description":"What host IP to bind the external port to."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-ports-hostport","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].ports[].hostPort","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Number of port to expose on the host.\nIf specified, this must be a valid port number, 0 < x < 65536.\nIf HostNetwork is specified, this must match ContainerPort.\nMost containers do not need this.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-ports-name","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].ports[].name","type":"string","required":false,"description":"If specified, this must be an IANA_SVC_NAME and unique within the pod. Each\nnamed port in a pod must have a unique name. Name for the port that can be\nreferred to by services."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-ports-protocol","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].ports[].protocol","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Protocol for port. Must be UDP, TCP, or SCTP.\nDefaults to \"TCP\".","metadata":["default: \"TCP\""]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Periodic probe of container service readiness.\nContainer will be removed from service endpoints if the probe fails.\nCannot be updated.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe-exec","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe.exec","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Exec specifies a command to execute in the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe-exec-command","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe.exec.command","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the working directory for the\ncommand  is root ('/') in the container's filesystem. The command is simply exec'd, it is\nnot run inside a shell, so traditional shell instructions ('|', etc) won't work. To use\na shell, you need to explicitly call out to that shell.\nExit status of 0 is treated as live/healthy and non-zero is unhealthy.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe-exec-command","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe.exec.command[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe-failurethreshold","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe.failureThreshold","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Minimum consecutive failures for the probe to be considered failed after having succeeded.\nDefaults to 3. Minimum value is 1.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe-grpc","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe.grpc","type":"object","required":false,"description":"GRPC specifies a GRPC HealthCheckRequest.","metadata":["requiredFields: port"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe-grpc-port","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe.grpc.port","type":"integer/int32","required":true,"description":"Port number of the gRPC service. Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe-grpc-service","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe.grpc.service","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Service is the name of the service to place in the gRPC HealthCheckRequest\n(see https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/health-checking.md).\n\nIf this is not specified, the default behavior is defined by gRPC.","metadata":["default: \"\""]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe-httpget","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe.httpGet","type":"object","required":false,"description":"HTTPGet specifies an HTTP GET request to perform.","metadata":["requiredFields: port"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe-httpget-host","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe.httpGet.host","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP. You probably want to set\n\"Host\" in httpHeaders instead."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe-httpget-httpheaders","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"Custom headers to set in the request. IANA_SVC_NAME.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe-httpget-scheme","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe.httpGet.scheme","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Scheme to use for connecting to the host.\nDefaults to int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe-periodseconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe.periodSeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"How
