Must be 1 for liveness and startup. Minimum value is 1.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe-tcpsocket","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe.tcpSocket","type":"object","required":false,"description":"TCPSocket specifies a connection to a TCP port.","metadata":["requiredFields: port"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe-tcpsocket-host","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe.tcpSocket.host","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Optional: Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe-tcpsocket-port","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe.tcpSocket.port","type":"int-or-string","required":true,"description":"Number or name of the port to access on the container.\nNumber must be in the range 1 to 65535.\nName must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"Optional duration in seconds the pod needs to terminate gracefully upon probe failure.\nThe grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes running in the pod are sent\na termination signal and the time when the processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal.\nSet this value longer than the expected cleanup time for your process.\nIf this value is nil, the pod's terminationGracePeriodSeconds will be used. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-readinessprobe-timeoutseconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].readinessProbe.timeoutSeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Number of seconds after which the probe times out.\nDefaults to 1 second. Minimum value is 1.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-resizepolicy","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].resizePolicy","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"Resources resize policy for the container.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-resizepolicy-resourcename","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].resizePolicy[].resourceName","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Name of the resource to which this resource resize policy applies.\nSupported values: cpu, memory."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-resizepolicy-restartpolicy","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].resizePolicy[].restartPolicy","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Restart policy to apply when specified resource is resized.\nIf not specified, it defaults to NotRequired."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-resources","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].resources","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Compute Resources required by this container.\nCannot be updated.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-resources-claims","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].resources.claims","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"Claims lists the names of resources, defined in spec.resourceClaims,\nthat are used by this container.\n\nThis field depends on the\nDynamicResourceAllocation feature gate.\n\nThis field is immutable. It can only be set for containers.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: map","x-kubernetes-list-map-keys: name"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-resources-claims-name","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].resources.claims[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Name must match the name of one entry in pod.spec.resourceClaims of\nthe Pod where this field is used. It makes that resource available\ninside a container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-resources-claims-request","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].resources.claims[].request","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Request is the name chosen for a request in the referenced claim.\nIf empty, everything from the claim is made available, otherwise\nonly the result of this request."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-resources-limits","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].resources.limits","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Limits describes the maximum amount of compute resources allowed.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-resources-limits-key","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].resources.limits.<key>","type":"int-or-string","required":false,"metadata":["pattern: ^(\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))(([KMGTPE]i)|[numkMGTPE]|([eE](\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))))?$","x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-resources-requests","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].resources.requests","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Requests describes the minimum amount of compute resources required.\nIf Requests is omitted for a container, it defaults to Limits if that is explicitly specified,\notherwise to an implementation-defined value. Requests cannot exceed Limits.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-resources-requests-key","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].resources.requests.<key>","type":"int-or-string","required":false,"metadata":["pattern: ^(\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))(([KMGTPE]i)|[numkMGTPE]|([eE](\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))))?$","x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-restartpolicy","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].restartPolicy","type":"string","required":false,"description":"RestartPolicy defines the restart behavior of individual containers in a pod.\nThis overrides the pod-level restart policy. When this field is not specified,\nthe restart behavior is defined by the Pod's restart policy and the container type.\nAdditionally, setting the RestartPolicy as \"Always\" for the init container will\nhave the following effect:\nthis init container will be continually restarted on\nexit until all regular containers have terminated. Once all regular\ncontainers have completed, all init containers with restartPolicy \"Always\"\nwill be shut down. This lifecycle differs from normal init containers and\nis often referred to as a \"sidecar\" container. Although this init\ncontainer still starts in the init container sequence, it does not wait\nfor the container to complete before proceeding to the next init\ncontainer. Instead, the next init container starts immediately after this\ninit container is started, or after any startupProbe has successfully\ncompleted."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-restartpolicyrules","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].restartPolicyRules","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the\ncontainer should be restarted on exit. The rules are evaluated in\norder. Once a rule matches a container exit condition, the remaining\nrules are ignored. If no rule matches the container exit condition,\nthe Container-level restart policy determines the whether the container\nis restarted or not. Constraints on the rules:\n- At most 20 rules are allowed.\n- Rules can have the same action.\n- Identical rules are not forbidden in validations.\nWhen rules are specified, container MUST set RestartPolicy explicitly\neven it if matches the Pod's RestartPolicy.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-restartpolicyrules-action","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].restartPolicyRules[].action","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Specifies the action taken on a container exit if the requirements\nare satisfied. The only possible value is \"Restart\" to restart the\ncontainer."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-restartpolicyrules-exitcodes","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].restartPolicyRules[].exitCodes","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Represents the exit codes to check on container exits.","metadata":["requiredFields: operator"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-restartpolicyrules-exitcodes-operator","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].restartPolicyRules[].exitCodes.operator","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Represents the relationship between the container exit code(s) and the\nspecified values. Possible values are:\n- In: the requirement is satisfied if the container exit code is in the\n  set of specified values.\n- NotIn: the requirement is satisfied if the container exit code is\n  not in the set of specified values."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-restartpolicyrules-exitcodes-values","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].restartPolicyRules[].exitCodes.values","type":"array<integer/int32>","required":false,"description":"Specifies the set of values to check for container exit codes.\nAt most 255 elements are allowed.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: set"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-restartpolicyrules-exitcodes-values","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].restartPolicyRules[].exitCodes.values[]","type":"integer/int32","required":true,"metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext","type":"object","required":false,"description":"SecurityContext defines the security options the container should be run with.\nIf set, the fields of SecurityContext override the equivalent fields of PodSecurityContext.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-allowprivilegeescalation","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"AllowPrivilegeEscalation controls whether a process can gain more\nprivileges than its parent process. This bool directly controls if\nthe no_new_privs flag will be set on the container process.\nAllowPrivilegeEscalation is true always when the container is:\n1) run as Privileged\n2) has CAP_SYS_ADMIN\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-apparmorprofile","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.appArmorProfile","type":"object","required":false,"description":"appArmorProfile is the AppArmor options to use by this container. If set, this profile\noverrides the pod's appArmorProfile.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["requiredFields: type"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-apparmorprofile-localhostprofile","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.appArmorProfile.localhostProfile","type":"string","required":false,"description":"localhostProfile indicates a profile loaded on the node that should be used.\nThe profile must be preconfigured on the node to work.\nMust match the loaded name of the profile.\nMust be set if and only if type is \"Localhost\"."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-apparmorprofile-type","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.appArmorProfile.type","type":"string","required":true,"description":"type indicates which kind of AppArmor profile will be applied.\nValid options are:\n  Localhost - a profile pre-loaded on the node.\n  RuntimeDefault - the container runtime's default profile.\n  Unconfined - no AppArmor enforcement."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-capabilities","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.capabilities","type":"object","required":false,"description":"The represent POSIX capabilities type"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-privileged","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.privileged","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Run container in privileged mode.\nProcesses in privileged containers are essentially equivalent to root on the host.\nDefaults to false.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-procmount","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.procMount","type":"string","required":false,"description":"procMount denotes the type of proc mount to use for the containers.\nThe default value is Default which uses the container runtime defaults for\nreadonly paths and masked paths.\nThis requires the ProcMountType feature flag to be enabled.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-readonlyrootfilesystem","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Whether this container has a read-only root filesystem.\nDefault is false.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-runasgroup","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.runAsGroup","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"The GID to run the entrypoint of the container process.\nUses runtime default if unset.\nMay also be set in PodSecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-runasnonroot","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.runAsNonRoot","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Indicates that the container must run as a non-root user.\nIf true, the Kubelet will validate the image at runtime to ensure that it\ndoes not run as UID 0 (root) and fail to start the container if it does.\nIf unset or false, no such validation will be performed.\nMay also be set in PodSecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-runasuser","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.runAsUser","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"The UID to run the entrypoint of the container process.\nDefaults to user specified in image metadata if unspecified.\nMay also be set in PodSecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-selinuxoptions","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.seLinuxOptions","type":"object","required":false,"description":"The SELinux context to be applied to the container.\nIf unspecified, the container runtime will allocate a random SELinux context for each\ncontainer.  May also be set in PodSecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-selinuxoptions-level","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.level","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Level is SELinux level label that applies to the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-selinuxoptions-role","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.role","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Role is a SELinux role label that applies to the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-selinuxoptions-type","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.type","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Type is a SELinux type label that applies to the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-selinuxoptions-user","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.user","type":"string","required":false,"description":"User is a SELinux user label that applies to the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-seccompprofile","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.seccompProfile","type":"object","required":false,"description":"The seccomp options to use by this container. If seccomp options are\nprovided at both the pod & container level, the container options\noverride the pod options.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["requiredFields: type"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-seccompprofile-localhostprofile","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.seccompProfile.localhostProfile","type":"string","required":false,"description":"localhostProfile indicates a profile defined in a file on the node should be used.\nThe profile must be preconfigured on the node to work.\nMust be a descending path, relative to the kubelet's configured seccomp profile location.\nMust be set if type is \"Localhost\". Must NOT be set for any other type."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-seccompprofile-type","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.seccompProfile.type","type":"string","required":true,"description":"type indicates which kind of seccomp profile will be applied.\nValid options are:\n\nLocalhost - a profile defined in a file on the node should be used.\nRuntimeDefault - the container runtime default profile should be used.\nUnconfined - no profile should be applied."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-windowsoptions","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.windowsOptions","type":"object","required":false,"description":"The Windows specific settings applied to all containers.\nIf unspecified, the options from the PodSecurityContext will be used.\nIf set in both SecurityContext and PodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is linux."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-windowsoptions-gmsacredentialspec","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.windowsOptions.gmsaCredentialSpec","type":"string","required":false,"description":"GMSACredentialSpec is where the GMSA admission webhook\n(https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/windows-gmsa) inlines the contents of the\nGMSA credential spec named by the GMSACredentialSpecName field."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-windowsoptions-gmsacredentialspecname","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.windowsOptions.gmsaCredentialSpecName","type":"string","required":false,"description":"GMSACredentialSpecName is the name of the GMSA credential spec to use."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-windowsoptions-hostprocess","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.windowsOptions.hostProcess","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"HostProcess determines if a container should be run as a 'Host Process' container.\nAll of a Pod's containers must have the same effective HostProcess value\n(it is not allowed to have a mix of HostProcess containers and non-HostProcess containers).\nIn addition, if HostProcess is true then HostNetwork must also be set to true."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-securitycontext-windowsoptions-runasusername","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].securityContext.windowsOptions.runAsUserName","type":"string","required":false,"description":"The UserName in Windows to run the entrypoint of the container process.\nDefaults to the user specified in image metadata if unspecified.\nMay also be set in PodSecurityContext. If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe","type":"object","required":false,"description":"StartupProbe indicates that the Pod has successfully initialized.\nIf specified, no other probes are executed until this completes successfully.\nIf this probe fails, the Pod will be restarted, just as if the livenessProbe failed.\nThis can be used to provide different probe parameters at the beginning of a Pod's lifecycle,\nwhen it might take a long time to load data or warm a cache, than during steady-state operation.\nThis cannot be updated.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-exec","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.exec","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Exec specifies a command to execute in the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-exec-command","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.exec.command","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the working directory for the\ncommand  is root ('/') in the container's filesystem. The command is simply exec'd, it is\nnot run inside a shell, so traditional shell instructions ('|', etc) won't work. To use\na shell, you need to explicitly call out to that shell.\nExit status of 0 is treated as live/healthy and non-zero is unhealthy.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-exec-command","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.exec.command[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-failurethreshold","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.failureThreshold","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Minimum consecutive failures for the probe to be considered failed after having succeeded.\nDefaults to 3. Minimum value is 1.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-grpc","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.grpc","type":"object","required":false,"description":"GRPC specifies a GRPC HealthCheckRequest.","metadata":["requiredFields: port"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-grpc-port","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.grpc.port","type":"integer/int32","required":true,"description":"Port number of the gRPC service. Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-grpc-service","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.grpc.service","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Service is the name of the service to place in the gRPC HealthCheckRequest\n(see https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/health-checking.md).\n\nIf this is not specified, the default behavior is defined by gRPC.","metadata":["default: \"\""]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-httpget","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.httpGet","type":"object","required":false,"description":"HTTPGet specifies an HTTP GET request to perform.","metadata":["requiredFields: port"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-httpget-host","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.httpGet.host","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP. header field name.\nThis will be canonicalized upon output, so case-variant names will be understood as the same header."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-httpget-httpheaders-value","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[].value","type":"string","required":true,"description":"The to access on the HTTP server."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-httpget-port","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.httpGet.port","type":"int-or-string","required":true,"description":"Name IANA_SVC_NAME.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-httpget-scheme","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.httpGet.scheme","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Scheme to use for connecting to the host.\nDefaults to HTTP."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-initialdelayseconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.initialDelaySeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Number of seconds after the container has started before liveness probes are initiated.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-periodseconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.periodSeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"How often (in seconds) to perform the probe.\nDefault to 10 seconds. Minimum value is 1.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-successthreshold","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.successThreshold","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Minimum consecutive successes for the probe to be considered successful after having failed.\nDefaults to 1. Must be 1 for liveness and startup. Minimum value is 1.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-tcpsocket","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.tcpSocket","type":"object","required":false,"description":"TCPSocket specifies a connection to a TCP port.","metadata":["requiredFields: port"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-tcpsocket-host","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.tcpSocket.host","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Optional: Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-tcpsocket-port","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.tcpSocket.port","type":"int-or-string","required":true,"description":"Number or name of the port to access on the container.\nNumber must be in the range 1 to 65535.\nName must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"Optional duration in seconds the pod needs to terminate gracefully upon probe failure.\nThe grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes running in the pod are sent\na termination signal and the time when the processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal.\nSet this value longer than the expected cleanup time for your process.\nIf this value is nil, the pod's terminationGracePeriodSeconds will be used. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-startupprobe-timeoutseconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].startupProbe.timeoutSeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Number of seconds after which the probe times out.\nDefaults to 1 second. Minimum value is 1.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-stdin","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].stdin","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Whether this container should allocate a buffer for stdin in the container runtime. If this\nis not set, reads from stdin in the container will always result in EOF.\nDefault is false."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-stdinonce","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].stdinOnce","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Whether the container runtime should close the stdin channel after it has been opened by\na single attach. When stdin is true the stdin stream will remain open across multiple attach\nsessions. If stdinOnce is set to true, stdin is opened on container start, is empty until the\nfirst client attaches to stdin, and then remains open and accepts data until the client disconnects,\nat which time stdin is closed and remains closed until the container is restarted. If this\nflag is false, a container processes that reads from stdin will never receive an EOF.\nDefault is false"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-terminationmessagepath","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].terminationMessagePath","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Optional: Path at which the file to which the container's termination message\nwill be written is mounted into the container's filesystem.\nMessage written is intended to be brief final status, such as an assertion failure message.\nWill be truncated by the node if greater than 4096 bytes. The total message length across\nall containers will be limited to 12kb.\nDefaults to /dev/termination-log.\nCannot be updated."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-terminationmessagepolicy","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].terminationMessagePolicy","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Indicate how the termination message should be populated. File will use the contents of\nterminationMessagePath to populate the container status message on both success and failure.\nFallbackToLogsOnError will use the last chunk of container log output if the termination\nmessage file is empty and the container exited with an error.\nThe log output is limited to 2048 bytes or 80 lines, whichever is smaller.\nDefaults to File.\nCannot be updated."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-tty","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].tty","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Whether this container should allocate a TTY for itself, also requires 'stdin' to be true.\nDefault is false."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-volumedevices","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].volumeDevices","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"volumeDevices devicePath"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-volumedevices-devicepath","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].volumeDevices[].devicePath","type":"string","required":true,"description":"devicePath is the path inside of the container that the device will be mapped to."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-volumedevices-name","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].volumeDevices[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"name must match the name of a persistentVolumeClaim in the pod"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-volumemounts","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].volumeMounts","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"Pod volumes to mount into the container's filesystem.\nCannot be updated.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: map","x-kubernetes-list-map-keys: mountPath"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-volumemounts-mountpath","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].volumeMounts[].mountPath","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Path within the container at which the volume should be mounted.  Must\nnot contain ':'."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-volumemounts-mountpropagation","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].volumeMounts[].mountPropagation","type":"string","required":false,"description":"mountPropagation determines how mounts are propagated from the host\nto container and the other way around.\nWhen not set, MountPropagationNone is used.\nThis field is beta in 1.10.\nWhen RecursiveReadOnly is set to IfPossible or to Enabled, MountPropagation must be None or unspecified\n(which defaults to None)."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-volumemounts-name","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].volumeMounts[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"This must match the Name of a Volume."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-volumemounts-readonly","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].volumeMounts[].readOnly","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Mounted read-only if true, read-write otherwise (false or unspecified).\nDefaults to false."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-volumemounts-recursivereadonly","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].volumeMounts[].recursiveReadOnly","type":"string","required":false,"description":"RecursiveReadOnly specifies whether read-only mounts should be handled\nrecursively.\n\nIf ReadOnly is false, this field has no meaning and must be unspecified.\n\nIf ReadOnly is true, and this field is set to Disabled, the mount is not made\nrecursively read-only.  If this field is set to IfPossible, the mount is made\nrecursively read-only, if it is supported by the container runtime.  If this\nfield is set to Enabled, the mount is made recursively read-only if it is\nsupported by the container runtime, otherwise the pod will not be started and\nan error will be generated to indicate the reason.\n\nIf this field is set to IfPossible or Enabled, MountPropagation must be set to\nNone (or be unspecified, which defaults to None).\n\nIf this field is not specified, it is treated as an equivalent of Disabled."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-volumemounts-subpath","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].volumeMounts[].subPath","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Path within the volume from which the container's volume should be mounted.\nDefaults to \"\" (volume's root)."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-volumemounts-subpathexpr","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].volumeMounts[].subPathExpr","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Expanded path within the volume from which the container's volume should be mounted.\nBehaves similarly to SubPath but environment variable references $(VAR_NAME) are expanded using the container's environment.\nDefaults to \"\" (volume's root).\nSubPathExpr and SubPath are mutually exclusive."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-containers-workingdir","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.containers[].workingDir","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Container's updated."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-dnsconfig","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.dnsConfig","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Specifies the DNS parameters of a pod.\nParameters specified here will be merged to the generated DNS\nconfiguration based on atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-dnsconfig-nameservers","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.dnsConfig.nameservers[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-dnsconfig-options","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.dnsConfig.options","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"A name.\nRequired."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-dnsconfig-options-value","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.dnsConfig.options[].value","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Value value."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-dnsconfig-searches","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.dnsConfig.searches","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"A list of DNS 'ClusterFirstWithHostNet'."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-enableservicelinks","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.enableServiceLinks","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"EnableServiceLinks indicates whether information about services should be injected into pod's\nenvironment variables, matching the syntax of Docker links.\nOptional: Defaults to true."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"List of ephemeral containers run in this pod. This list cannot be specified when\ncreating a pod, and it cannot be modified by updating the pod spec. In order to add an\nephemeral container to an existing pod, use the pod's ephemeralcontainers subresource.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: map","x-kubernetes-list-map-keys: name"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-args","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].args","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"Arguments to the entrypoint.\nThe image's CMD is used if this is not provided.\nVariable references $(VAR_NAME) are expanded using the container's environment. If a variable\ncannot be resolved, the reference in the input string will be unchanged. Double $$ are reduced\nto a single $, which allows for escaping the $(VAR_NAME) syntax: i.e. \"$$(VAR_NAME)\" will\nproduce the string literal \"$(VAR_NAME)\". Escaped references will never be expanded, regardless\nof whether the variable exists or not. Cannot be updated.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-command-argument-container/#running-a-command-in-a-shell","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-args","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].args[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-command","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].command","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"Entrypoint array. Not executed within a shell.\nThe image's ENTRYPOINT is used if this is not provided.\nVariable references $(VAR_NAME) are expanded using the container's environment. If a variable\ncannot be resolved, the reference in the input string will be unchanged. Double $$ are reduced\nto a single $, which allows for escaping the $(VAR_NAME) syntax: i.e. \"$$(VAR_NAME)\" will\nproduce the string literal \"$(VAR_NAME)\". Escaped references will never be expanded, regardless\nof whether the variable exists or not. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-livenessprobe-timeoutseconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].livenessProbe.timeoutSeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Number of seconds after which the probe times out.\nDefaults to 1 second. Possible values are:\n- In: the requirement is satisfied if the container exit code is in the\n  set of specified values.\n- NotIn: the requirement is satisfied if the container exit code is\n  not in the set of specified values."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-restartpolicyrules-exitcodes-values","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].restartPolicyRules[].exitCodes.values","type":"array<integer/int32>","required":false,"description":"Specifies the set of values to check for container exit codes.\nAt most 255 elements are allowed.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: set"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-restartpolicyrules-exitcodes-values","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].restartPolicyRules[].exitCodes.values[]","type":"integer/int32","required":true,"metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Optional: SecurityContext defines the security options the ephemeral container should be run with.\nIf set, the fields of SecurityContext override the equivalent fields of PodSecurityContext."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-allowprivilegeescalation","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"AllowPrivilegeEscalation controls whether a process can gain more\nprivileges than its parent process. This bool directly controls if\nthe no_new_privs flag will be set on the container process.\nAllowPrivilegeEscalation is true always when the container is:\n1) run as Privileged\n2) has CAP_SYS_ADMIN\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-apparmorprofile","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.appArmorProfile","type":"object","required":false,"description":"appArmorProfile is the AppArmor options to use by this container. If set, this profile\noverrides the pod's appArmorProfile.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["requiredFields: type"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-apparmorprofile-localhostprofile","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.appArmorProfile.localhostProfile","type":"string","required":false,"description":"localhostProfile indicates a profile loaded on the node that should be used.\nThe profile must be preconfigured on the node to work.\nMust match the loaded name of the profile.\nMust be set if and only if type is \"Localhost\"."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-apparmorprofile-type","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.appArmorProfile.type","type":"string","required":true,"description":"type indicates which kind of AppArmor profile will be applied.\nValid options are:\n  Localhost - a profile pre-loaded on the node.\n  RuntimeDefault - the container runtime's default profile.\n  Unconfined - no AppArmor enforcement."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-capabilities","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.capabilities","type":"object","required":false,"description":"The capabilities to add/drop when running containers.\nDefaults to the default set of capabilities granted by the container runtime.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-capabilities-add","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.capabilities.add","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"Added capabilities","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-capabilities-add","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.capabilities.add[]","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Capability represent POSIX capabilities type"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-capabilities-drop","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.capabilities.drop","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"Removed capabilities","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-capabilities-drop","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.capabilities.drop[]","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Capability represent POSIX capabilities type"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-privileged","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.privileged","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Run container in privileged mode.\nProcesses in privileged containers are essentially equivalent to root on the host.\nDefaults to false.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-procmount","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.procMount","type":"string","required":false,"description":"procMount denotes the type of proc mount to use for the containers.\nThe default value is Default which uses the container runtime defaults for\nreadonly paths and masked paths.\nThis requires the ProcMountType feature flag to be enabled.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-readonlyrootfilesystem","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Whether this container has a read-only root filesystem.\nDefault is false.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-runasgroup","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.runAsGroup","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"The GID to run the entrypoint of the container process.\nUses runtime default if unset.\nMay also be set in PodSecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-runasnonroot","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.runAsNonRoot","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Indicates that the container must run as a non-root user.\nIf true, the Kubelet will validate the image at runtime to ensure that it\ndoes not run as UID 0 (root) and fail to start the container if it does.\nIf unset or false, no such validation will be performed.\nMay also be set in PodSecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-runasuser","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.runAsUser","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"The UID to run the entrypoint of the container process.\nDefaults to user specified in image metadata if unspecified.\nMay also be set in PodSecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-selinuxoptions","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.seLinuxOptions","type":"object","required":false,"description":"The SELinux context to be applied to the container.\nIf unspecified, the container runtime will allocate a random SELinux context for each\ncontainer.  May also be set in PodSecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-selinuxoptions-level","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.level","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Level is SELinux level label that applies to the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-selinuxoptions-role","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.role","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Role is a SELinux role label that applies to the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-selinuxoptions-type","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.type","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Type is a SELinux type label that applies to the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-selinuxoptions-user","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.user","type":"string","required":false,"description":"User is a SELinux user label that applies to the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-seccompprofile","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.seccompProfile","type":"object","required":false,"description":"The seccomp options to use by this container. If seccomp options are\nprovided at both the pod & container level, the container options\noverride the pod options.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["requiredFields: type"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-seccompprofile-localhostprofile","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.seccompProfile.localhostProfile","type":"string","required":false,"description":"localhostProfile indicates a profile defined in a file on the node should be used.\nThe profile must be preconfigured on the node to work.\nMust be a descending path, relative to the kubelet's configured seccomp profile location.\nMust be set if type is \"Localhost\". Must NOT be set for any other type."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-seccompprofile-type","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.seccompProfile.type","type":"string","required":true,"description":"type indicates which kind of seccomp profile will be applied.\nValid options are:\n\nLocalhost - a profile defined in a file on the node should be used.\nRuntimeDefault - the container runtime default profile should be used.\nUnconfined - no profile should be applied."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-windowsoptions","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.windowsOptions","type":"object","required":false,"description":"The Windows specific settings applied to all containers.\nIf unspecified, the options from the PodSecurityContext will be used.\nIf set in both SecurityContext and PodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is linux."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-windowsoptions-gmsacredentialspec","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.windowsOptions.gmsaCredentialSpec","type":"string","required":false,"description":"GMSACredentialSpec is where the GMSA admission webhook\n(https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/windows-gmsa) inlines the contents of the\nGMSA credential spec named by the GMSACredentialSpecName field."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-windowsoptions-gmsacredentialspecname","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.windowsOptions.gmsaCredentialSpecName","type":"string","required":false,"description":"GMSACredentialSpecName is the name of the GMSA credential spec to use."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-windowsoptions-hostprocess","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.windowsOptions.hostProcess","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"HostProcess determines if a container should be run as a 'Host Process' container.\nAll of a Pod's containers must have the same effective HostProcess value\n(it is not allowed to have a mix of HostProcess containers and non-HostProcess containers).\nIn addition, if HostProcess is true then HostNetwork must also be set to true."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-securitycontext-windowsoptions-runasusername","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].securityContext.windowsOptions.runAsUserName","type":"string","required":false,"description":"The UserName in Windows to run the entrypoint of the container process.\nDefaults to the user specified in image metadata if unspecified.\nMay also be set in PodSecurityContext. If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Probes are not allowed for ephemeral containers."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-exec","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.exec","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Exec specifies a command to execute in the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-exec-command","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.exec.command","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the working directory for the\ncommand  is root ('/') in the container's filesystem. The command is simply exec'd, it is\nnot run inside a shell, so traditional shell instructions ('|', etc) won't work. To use\na shell, you need to explicitly call out to that shell.\nExit status of 0 is treated as live/healthy and non-zero is unhealthy.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-exec-command","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.exec.command[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-failurethreshold","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.failureThreshold","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Minimum consecutive failures for the probe to be considered failed after having succeeded.\nDefaults to 3. Minimum value is 1.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-grpc","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.grpc","type":"object","required":false,"description":"GRPC specifies a GRPC HealthCheckRequest.","metadata":["requiredFields: port"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-grpc-port","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.grpc.port","type":"integer/int32","required":true,"description":"Port number of the gRPC service. Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-grpc-service","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.grpc.service","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Service is the name of the service to place in the gRPC HealthCheckRequest\n(see https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/health-checking.md).\n\nIf this is not specified, the default behavior is defined by gRPC.","metadata":["default: \"\""]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-httpget","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.httpGet","type":"object","required":false,"description":"HTTPGet specifies an HTTP GET request to perform.","metadata":["requiredFields: port"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-httpget-host","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.httpGet.host","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP. You probably want to set\n\"Host\" in httpHeaders instead."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-httpget-httpheaders","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"Custom headers to set in the request. atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-httpget-httpheaders-name","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"The header field name.\nThis will be canonicalized upon output, so case-variant names will be understood as the same header."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-httpget-httpheaders-value","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[].value","type":"string","required":true,"description":"The header field value"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-httpget-path","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.httpGet.path","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Path to access on the HTTP server."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-httpget-port","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.httpGet.port","type":"int-or-string","required":true,"description":"Name or number of the port to access on the container.\nNumber must be in the range 1 to 65535.\nName must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-httpget-scheme","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.httpGet.scheme","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Scheme to use for connecting to the host.\nDefaults to HTTP."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-initialdelayseconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.initialDelaySeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Number of seconds after the container has started before liveness probes are initiated.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-periodseconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.periodSeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"How often (in seconds) to perform the probe.\nDefault to 10 seconds. Minimum value is 1.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-successthreshold","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.successThreshold","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Minimum consecutive successes for the probe to be considered successful after having failed.\nDefaults to 1. Must be 1 for liveness and startup. Minimum value is 1.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-tcpsocket","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.tcpSocket","type":"object","required":false,"description":"TCPSocket specifies a connection to a TCP port.","metadata":["requiredFields: port"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-tcpsocket-host","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.tcpSocket.host","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Optional: Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-tcpsocket-port","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.tcpSocket.port","type":"int-or-string","required":true,"description":"Number or name of the port to access on the container.\nNumber must be in the range 1 to 65535.\nName must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"Optional duration in seconds the pod needs to terminate gracefully upon probe failure.\nThe grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes running in the pod are sent\na termination signal and the time when the processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal.\nSet this value longer than the expected cleanup time for your process.\nIf this value is nil, the pod's terminationGracePeriodSeconds will be used. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-timeoutseconds","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.timeoutSeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Number of seconds after which the probe times out.\nDefaults to 1 second. Minimum value is 1.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-stdin","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].stdin","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Whether this container should allocate a buffer for stdin in the container runtime. If this\nis not set, reads from stdin in the container will always result in EOF.\nDefault is false."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-stdinonce","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].stdinOnce","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Whether the container runtime should close the stdin channel after it has been opened by\na single attach. When stdin is true the stdin stream will remain open across multiple attach\nsessions. If stdinOnce is set to true, stdin is opened on container start, is empty until the\nfirst client attaches to stdin, and then remains open and accepts data until the client disconnects,\nat which time stdin is closed and remains closed until the container is restarted. If this\nflag is false, a container processes that reads from stdin will never receive an EOF.\nDefault is false"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-targetcontainername","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].targetContainerName","type":"string","required":false,"description":"If set, the name of the container from PodSpec that this ephemeral container targets.\nThe ephemeral container will be run in the namespaces (IPC, PID, etc) of this container.\nIf not set then the ephemeral container uses the namespaces configured in the Pod spec.\n\nThe container runtime must implement support for this feature. If the runtime does not\nsupport namespace targeting then the result of setting this field is undefined."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-terminationmessagepath","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].terminationMessagePath","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Optional: Path at which the file to which the container's termination message\nwill be written is mounted into the container's filesystem.\nMessage written is intended to be brief final status, such as an assertion failure message.\nWill be truncated by the node if greater than 4096 bytes. The total message length across\nall containers will be limited to 12kb.\nDefaults to /dev/termination-log.\nCannot be updated."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-terminationmessagepolicy","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].terminationMessagePolicy","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Indicate how the termination message should be populated. File will use the contents of\nterminationMessagePath to populate the container status message on both success and failure.\nFallbackToLogsOnError will use the last chunk of container log output if the termination\nmessage file is empty and the container exited with an error.\nThe log output is limited to 2048 bytes or 80 lines, whichever is smaller.\nDefaults to File.\nCannot be updated."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-tty","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].tty","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Whether this container should allocate a TTY for itself, also requires 'stdin' to be true.\nDefault is false."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-volumedevices","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].volumeDevices","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"volumeDevices is the list of block devices to be used by the container.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: map","x-kubernetes-list-map-keys: devicePath"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-volumedevices-devicepath","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].volumeDevices[].devicePath","type":"string","required":true,"description":"devicePath is the path inside of the container that the device will be mapped to."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-volumedevices-name","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].volumeDevices[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"name must match the name of a persistentVolumeClaim in the pod"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-volumemounts","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].volumeMounts","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"Pod volumes to mount into the container's filesystem. Subpath mounts are not allowed for ephemeral containers.\nCannot be updated.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: map","x-kubernetes-list-map-keys: mountPath"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-volumemounts-mountpath","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].volumeMounts[].mountPath","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Path within the container at which the volume should be mounted. If this field is set to IfPossible, the mount is made\nrecursively read-only, if it is supported by the container runtime.  If this\nfield is set to Enabled, the mount is made recursively read-only if it is\nsupported by the container runtime, otherwise the pod will not be started and\nan error will be generated to indicate the reason.\n\nIf this field is set to IfPossible or Enabled, MountPropagation must be set to\nNone (or be unspecified, which defaults to None).\n\nIf this field is not specified, it is treated as an equivalent of Disabled."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-volumemounts-subpath","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].volumeMounts[].subPath","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Path within the volume from which the container's volume should be mounted.\nDefaults to \"\" (volume's root)."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-ephemeralcontainers-volumemounts-subpathexpr","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.ephemeralContainers[].volumeMounts[].subPathExpr","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Expanded path within the volume from which the container's volume should be mounted.\nBehaves similarly to SubPath but environment variable references $(VAR_NAME) are expanded using the container's address of the host file entry."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-hostipc","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.hostIPC","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Use the host's ipc namespace.\nOptional: Default to false."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-hostnetwork","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.hostNetwork","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Host networking requested for this pod. Use the host's network namespace.\nWhen using HostNetwork you should specify ports so the scheduler is aware.\nWhen `hostNetwork` is true, specified `hostPort` fields in port definitions must match `containerPort`,\nand unspecified `hostPort` fields in port definitions are defaulted to match `containerPort`.\nDefault to false."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-hostpid","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.hostPID","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Use the host's pid namespace.\nOptional: Default to false."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-hostusers","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.hostUsers","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Use the host's user namespace.\nOptional: Default to true.\nIf set to true or not present, the pod will be run in the host user namespace, useful\nfor when the pod needs a feature only available to the host user namespace, such as\nloading a kernel module with CAP_SYS_MODULE.\nWhen set to false, a new userns is created for the pod. Setting false is useful for\nmitigating container breakout vulnerabilities even allowing users to run their\ncontainers as root without actually having root privileges on the host.\nThis field is alpha-level and is only honored by servers that enable the UserNamespacesSupport feature."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-hostname","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.hostname","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Specifies the hostname of the Pod\nIf not specified, the pod's hostname will be set to a system-defined value."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-hostnameoverride","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.hostnameOverride","type":"string","required":false,"description":"HostnameOverride specifies an explicit override for the pod's hostname as perceived by the pod.\nThis field only specifies the pod's hostname and does not affect its DNS records.\nWhen this field is set to a non-empty string:\n- It takes precedence over the values set in `hostname` and `subdomain`.\n- The Pod's hostname will be set to this value.\n- `setHostnameAsFQDN` must be nil or set to false.\n- `hostNetwork` must be set to false.\n\nThis field must be a valid DNS subdomain as defined in RFC 1123 and contain at most 64 characters.\nRequires the HostnameOverride feature gate to be enabled."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-imagepullsecrets","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.imagePullSecrets","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"ImagePullSecrets is an optional list of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any of the images used by this PodSpec.\nIf specified, these secrets will be passed to individual puller implementations for them to use.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: map","x-kubernetes-list-map-keys: name"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-imagepullsecrets-name","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.imagePullSecrets[].name","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Name of the referent.\nThis field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is\nallowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are\nalmost certainly wrong.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names","metadata":["default: \"\""]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-initcontainers","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.initContainers","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"List of initialization containers belonging to the pod.\nInit containers are executed in order prior to containers being started. If any\ninit container fails, the pod is considered to have failed and is handled according\nto its restartPolicy. The name for an init container or normal container must be\nunique among all containers.\nInit containers may not have Lifecycle actions, Readiness probes, Liveness probes, or Startup probes.\nThe resourceRequirements of an init container are taken into account during scheduling\nby finding the highest request/limit for each resource type, and then using the max of\nthat value or the sum of the normal containers. Limits are applied to init containers\nin a similar fashion.\nInit containers cannot currently be added or removed.\nCannot be updated.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/init-containers/","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: map","x-kubernetes-list-map-keys: name"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-extrapodspec-initcontainers-args","path":"spec.extraPodSpec.initContainers[].args","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"Arguments to the entrypoint.\nThe container image's CMD is used if this is not provided.\nVariable references $(VAR_NAME) are expanded using the container's environment. If a variable\ncannot be resolved, the reference in the input string will be unchanged. Double $$ are reduced\nto a single $, which allows for escaping the $(VAR_NAME) syntax: i.e. \"$$(VAR_NAME)\" will\nproduce the string literal \"$(VAR_NAME)\". Escaped references will never be expanded, regardless\nof whether the variable exists or not. The Pod's termination grace period countdown begins before the\nPreStop hook is executed. Regardless of the outcome of the handler, the\ncontainer will eventually terminate within the Pod's termination grace\nperiod (unless delayed by finalizers).
